Compiled research file

Every Public André Mack Wine Recommendation We Could Verify

Search André Hueston Mack's public wine trail by bottle, price, score, source, verdict, celebrity wine, budget pick, panel-tasting context, and buy-wine link.

Fan-made research index. Not affiliated with or endorsed by André Hueston Mack, Bon Appetit, Delectable, or the linked publishers.

A fan-made field guide to Mack's public wine trail.

This page pulls public reviews, Bon Appetit tastings, Delectable notes, and panel appearances into one searchable guide. Source labels separate solo Mack tasting notes from judging-panel context, because wine people notice that kind of thing.

Live tasting An Evening With Andre Hueston Mack
51 winesTap to filter

Affordable wins from under-$15 shelves, Costco runs, Trader Joe's finds, boxed pours, and grocery-store surprises that Mack thought delivered.

30 winesTap to filter

Star-backed bottles that earned real attention from Mack, including plenty that outperformed the usual celebrity-wine expectations.

39 winesTap to filter

A 2026 New York panel tasting revisits the Paris-tasting idea through ranked Chardonnay, Syrah, Chenin Blanc, and Cabernet Sauvignon flights.

5 winesTap to filter

The rare public bottles where Mack's score reached 9.5 or higher; a tight highlight reel rather than a broad ranking.

34 winesTap to filter

Bottles that drew thumbs-down notes from Mack, useful for seeing where the tasting record gets skeptical instead of celebratory.

332 wines ▾ FILTER

Orison Pipa, Alentejano / Portugal

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$$ standard Portugal Delectable Highest score
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

This is admiration in bottle form, with Mack reacting as much to the people behind it as to the pour. The captured note, "Love It When Friends Do Big Things!!", makes the review feel generous, personal, and fired up. The 9.8 score says this was not just a polite nod; Mack clearly put it near the top of his public notes.

19 Crimes Cali Red, Snoop Dogg

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$ value USA Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack treats the 19 Crimes packaging as part of the experience, from the matte bottle to the Snoop Dogg cork. In the wine he finds sulfur, hair-salon aromas, decent color with a strange purple cast, and a sweet cupcake-like impression.

Rose · Video tasting

19 Crimes Cali Rose, Snoop Dogg

Mack Mack thought captured
👎🏾 Thumbs down from Mack notes $$$ standard USA Bon Appetit Celebrity wine
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack is unsure what grapes are in 19 Crimes Cali Rose and flags its low 10.5% alcohol as a possible sweetness signal. After the living-label gimmick, he sees a slightly neon color, smells watermelon and raspberry, then finds it a little too sweet with an artificial-sweetener impression. He says it tastes more like White Zinfandel than dry rose and rates it with a teardrop face.

Other / blend · Video tasting

Adelante Malbec, Mendoza

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$ value Argentina Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack calls Mendoza a good place for Malbec and likes the natural dark ruby-purple color. He gets raspberry, tobacco, smoke, earth, refinement, full tannin, moderate alcohol, black fruit, licorice, a long finish, and says the wine catches him off guard in a good way.

Other / blend · Video tasting

Alabama: Morgan Creek Vineyards Regal Red

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$$ standard Other / mixed regions Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack's captured note is direct: "Mack finds it sweet, ripe, acidic, slightly nutty, and sherry-like from Muscadine and residual sugar; not an everyday pour for him, but cool and fun as a local Alabama wine.". The tone is quick, personal, and more about the moment of the bottle than a formal grid of fruit, acid, and tannin. There is no separate score attached here, so the note has to carry the whole read.

Alternative / fruit wine · Video tasting

Alaska: Blueberry wine

Mack Mack thought captured
👎🏾 Thumbs down from Mack notes $$$ standard Other / mixed regions Bon Appetit Pushed back
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack's captured note is direct: "Mack says it smells partly like blueberries but also like disinfectant or a sterile hospital room; he finds it one-dimensional and says he would not drink it, while respecting the artistry of making wine from what grows there.". The tone is quick, personal, and more about the moment of the bottle than a formal grid of fruit, acid, and tannin. There is no separate score attached here, so the note has to carry the whole read.

Other / blend · Video tasting

Altos d'Oliva Tempranillo 2018

Mack Mack thought captured
👎🏾 Thumbs down from Mack notes $$ value Spain Bon Appetit Pushed back
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack catches volatile acidity in the first bottle, rejects it as flawed, then uses the backup to show how bottle variation works. The sound bottle gives stewed plum, raspberry, and boysenberry, and he stresses that restaurant tasting pours are meant to catch exactly this kind of fault.

Other / blend · Video tasting

Altos d'Oliva White Blend 2019

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$ value Spain Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack identifies Altos d'Oliva as a Spanish white blend of Airen and Sauvignon Blanc. He gets baked apple, lemon, unusually floral aromatics, muscat-gummy flavor, creaminess, ripeness, and says it is not bad if you are strapped for cash and looking for a last bottle of the night.

Other / blend · Video tasting

Arizona: noble-grape red wine; exact label not exposed in transcript

Mack Mack thought captured
👎🏾 Thumbs down from Mack notes $$$ standard Other / mixed regions Bon Appetit Pushed back
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack's captured note is direct: "Mack recognizes an Arizona noble-grape style with Rhone-like sandy-soil richness, clove, cinnamon, chocolate-covered cherry, and a short caramelized finish, but he flags acetone as a fault he struggles to get past.". The tone is quick, personal, and more about the moment of the bottle than a formal grid of fruit, acid, and tannin. There is no separate score attached here, so the note has to carry the whole read.

Arkansas: Chateau aux Arc Savant, Arkansas white table wine

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$$ standard Other / mixed regions Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack's captured note is direct: "Mack is surprised that this listed white table wine looks red; he gets Muscadine musk, vanilla, cinnamon, caramelized sugar, honeyed thickness, acidity, and a liquified-fruitcake quality that needs food.". The tone is quick, personal, and more about the moment of the bottle than a formal grid of fruit, acid, and tannin. There is no separate score attached here, so the note has to carry the whole read.

Champagne / sparkling · Video tasting

Armand de Brignac / Ace of Spades Champagne, Jay-Z

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$$$$ splurge France Bon Appetit Celebrity wine
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack opens with Armand de Brignac, aka Ace of Spades by Jay-Z, and immediately notes the flashy packaging. He confirms it is real Champagne, then gets yeast, bread dough, slight grassiness, lemon-lime, and balance. He says it is actually really good, but at $350 he questions the lack of vintage and wants more age and nuance for the money.

Avaline White Wine Blend, Cameron Diaz

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$ value USA Bon Appetit Celebrity wine
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack identifies Avaline as a Spanish white blend of Xarel-lo, Macabeo, and Malvasia bottled in California, with no visible vintage. He gets pear, apple, applesauce, strong acidity, grassiness, freshness, oceanic melon-like character, and says it is tasty and refreshing but not good value at $24 because similar wines cost half as much.

Champagne / sparkling · Video tasting

Babe Rose, canned sparkling rose

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$ value USA Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack likes Babe's alternative canned packaging in principle, especially for pools, hikes, and recyclable no-glass settings. In the glass he finds a dark red-tinged color, BO funk, bad antique-store aromas, a rush of sweetness amplified by bubbles, and calls it unpleasant to him and too sweet, even though he can see why some people like it.

Backstory California Cabernet Sauvignon

Mack Mack thought captured
👎🏾 Thumbs down from Mack notes $$ value USA Bon Appetit Pushed back
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack notes that Backstory is more specific than the broad American Cabernet because it names California and the grape. He gets black currant, licorice, anise, and too much artificial-seeming vanilla, then calls the palate green and vegetal enough to be a hard pass.

Rose · Video tasting

Baela Txakoli Rose, Basque region, Spain

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$ value Spain Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack places Baela Txakoli Rose in Spain's Basque region and calls that area one of his favorite sources for rose. He notes the Hondarrabi grape, trapped gas, and traditional high pour, then finds herbaceous grass, pepper, some fruit under the green notes, and a beautiful, food-exciting contrast to the rest of the lineup.

Other / blend · Video tasting

Bandit Merlot, California

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $ budget USA Bon Appetit Budget pick
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack treats Bandit Merlot as alternative Tetra Pak packaging and notices Old World-like forest floor and dried leaves before the palate turns clearly California: ripe, round, rich, and a little light. It reminds him of Merlot-heavy Right Bank Bordeaux, overdelivers nuance for seven dollars, but is a bit too ripe and alcohol-heavy.

Italian red · Video tasting

Barbera d'Asti, Italy

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $ budget Italy Bon Appetit Budget pick
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack's captured note is direct: "For Friendsgiving, Mack wants something fun, energetic, budget-friendly, and easy to keep flowing. He picks Barbera d'Asti for medium body, acidity, round rich fruit, crowd-pleasing drinkability, and Italian value.". The tone is quick, personal, and more about the moment of the bottle than a formal grid of fruit, acid, and tannin. There is no separate score attached here, so the note has to carry the whole read.

Pinot Noir · Video tasting

Barda Pinot Noir, Patagonia, Argentina

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $ budget Argentina Bon Appetit Budget pick
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack uses Barda to show that Argentina can do more than Malbec. He finds intense, powerful minerality, plum, bittersweet chocolate, cherry, fresh-cut grass, integrated acid, dense wet-rock character, high-elevation cool-night freshness, and true Pinot Noir funk, then says the bottle is something different for Patagonia even if it is not the region's cheapest example.

Riesling · Video tasting

Barefoot On Tap California Riesling

Mack Mack thought captured
👎🏾 Thumbs down from Mack notes $ budget USA Bon Appetit Pushed back
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack expects Barefoot On Tap Riesling to be sweet because it is 8% alcohol and does find apple, pear, and sweetness. It does not taste bad, but it lacks the minerality and acid Riesling needs, so he says California can do better and would rather buy a good German Riesling.

Rose · Video tasting

Barefoot Pink Moscato, California

Mack Mack thought captured
👎🏾 Thumbs down from Mack notes $$ value USA Bon Appetit Pushed back
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack prepares himself for sweetness because Barefoot Pink Moscato says 'deliciously sweet' on the label. He gets potpourri bubblegum and heavy scented oil, finds it less cloying than expected but hard to drink beyond one glass, and says it would need the cheese tray if he were at a party.

Aromatic / other white · Video tasting

Barefoot Pinot Grigio

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$ value USA Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack is skeptical of the medal sticker, lack of vintage, and broad American designation on Barefoot Pinot Grigio. He smells grass, lemon-lime, and cotton candy, then finds it sweeter than he likes, like fruit-cocktail juice: not bad, but maybe a last-bottle-of-the-night wine.

Italian red · Pairing

Barolo / Nebbiolo, Piedmont

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $ budget Italy Bon Appetit Budget pick
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack pairs Barolo with Little Caesars pepperoni pizza, using Nebbiolo's tar, rose petal, wild berries, brickish maturity, and complexity as a high-low contrast. He calls it a beautiful pairing and the ultimate pizza wine, even for much more upscale pizza.

Beringer Cabernet Sauvignon

Mack Mack thought captured
👎🏾 Thumbs down from Mack notes $$ value USA Bon Appetit Pushed back
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack flags the broad American Cabernet label and lack of vintage as quality warnings because the wine can be sourced widely and blended across years. In the glass he calls it flabby, low-acid, sweet in the middle, and not what he wants from Cabernet.

Aromatic / other white · Video tasting

Bieler Family "Daisy" Pinot Grigio 2019

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$ value USA Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack reads Daisy as a Washington Pinot Grigio that includes other grapes under the varietal-labeling rules. He gets fresh-cut grass, honeydew melon, Sauvignon Blanc-like herbaceousness, Riesling-like petrol, and a lemon-lime vinaigrette feeling, calling it pleasant, different, and a great variation on Pinot Grigio.

Chardonnay · Video tasting

Big Sipper California Chardonnay

Mack Mack thought captured
👎🏾 Thumbs down from Mack notes $ budget USA Bon Appetit Pushed back
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack reads Big Sipper as a no-vintage California Chardonnay and gets the expected oak, vanilla, butter, and toast. At $13 he says it is not badly made and does deliver the California Chardonnay effect, but it is boring, gives very little, and is not something he would tell people about.

Champagne / sparkling · Video tasting

Billecart-Salmon Brut Rose Champagne

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$ value France Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack's captured note is direct: "For a quiet New Year's celebration, Mack goes back to champagne but chooses rose. He highlights the salmon color, minerality, raspberry, strawberry, rarity, and special-occasion feel.". The tone is quick, personal, and more about the moment of the bottle than a formal grid of fruit, acid, and tannin. There is no separate score attached here, so the note has to carry the whole read.

Cabernet / Bordeaux · Video tasting

Black Box Cabernet Sauvignon, Chile

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $ budget Chile Bon Appetit Budget pick
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack credits Black Box Cabernet for helping market boxed wine as premium and likes that it includes a vintage. He gets violets, leather, green pepper, decent acid, and good structure, though it feels a little light for Cabernet and carries a slightly chemical edge; he rates it interesting rather than a must-buy.

Champagne / sparkling · Video tasting

Black Elephant Vintners sparkling Chardonnay / Pinot Noir, South Africa

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$$ standard South Africa Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack introduces Black Elephant Vintners as a South African MCC, the country's version of traditional-method sparkling wine, made from Chardonnay and Pinot Noir. He finds straw-pear color, yeasty toast, ripe red apple, pear, cool-climate low-alcohol freshness, and calls it stellar enough to make him want to drink more South African sparkling wine.

Syrah / Rhone · Video tasting

Block Red Wine Shiraz, Australia

Mack Mack thought captured
👎🏾 Thumbs down from Mack notes $ budget Australia Bon Appetit Pushed back
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack uses Block Shiraz to talk about Australia's success with the Shiraz name, then finds white pepper, raspberry, currant, and nutmeg under heavy alcohol. The wine tastes maderized, raisiny, old in a bad way, and possibly heat damaged, lacking the lift and peppery pep he expects from Shiraz.

Aromatic / other white · Video tasting

Bodegas Barbadillo "Castillo San Diego" Palomino Fino 2019

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$ value Italy Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack is drawn to Castillo San Diego because Palomino Fino is tied to Sherry and appeals to his wine-geek side. He gets oxidative, caramelized aromas, more body than expected, fullness, richness, decent acid, and a caramel-popcorn finish, calling it pure joy from exploration and a rockstar bottle.

👎🏾 Thumbs down from Mack notes $$ value USA Bon Appetit Pushed back

Mack sees Bota Box Nighthawk Black's award language but immediately finds dank wet cardboard and refuses to keep tasting it. He says it tastes manufactured, lab-like, plush in the wrong way for Pinot Noir, not worth $19, and something shoppers should skip.

Pinot Noir · Video tasting

Burn Cottage Moonlight Race Pinot Noir 2020, Central Otago, New Zealand

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$$ standard New Zealand Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack says Burn Cottage Moonlight Race shows why Central Otago Pinot Noir is a legend inside the Pinot world. He sees a deeper grape-juice color, then gets cherry cola, tobacco, cherry pie, ripe fruit, savory layers, truffles, leather, rosemary, thyme, herbaceousness, electric energy, more tannic structure than Burgundy, and says it drinks like a much more expensive bottle.

Other / blend · Video tasting

Caduceus Le Cortigiane Oneste 2017, Maynard James Keenan

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$$ standard Other / mixed regions Bon Appetit Celebrity wine
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack treats Caduceus Le Cortigiane Oneste as a highly requested Maynard James Keenan wine and notes the 2017 bottle maturity. He gets brickish color, leather, smoke, dried plum, cranberry, strong acidity from Barbera, Merlot-driven plumminess, balance, Old World aromatics, and says it is really good, worth $50, and convincing enough to be a blind-tasting ringer for Italian Barbera.

Cabernet / Bordeaux · Video tasting

California: BV Vineyards Cabernet Sauvignon, Napa Valley

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$$ standard USA Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack's captured note is direct: "Mack reads this as classic California Cabernet: campfire smoke, raspberry, violets, cranberry, rich tannins, bitter chocolate, cassis, concentration, complexity, and elegance; he calls it familiar, comforting, and like a warm hug.". The tone is quick, personal, and more about the moment of the bottle than a formal grid of fruit, acid, and tannin. There is no separate score attached here, so the note has to carry the whole read.

Campo Viejo Rioja Reserva 2015

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$ value Spain Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack calls Rioja Spain's most famous region and ties it to Tempranillo. He finds bottle maturity in the brickish color, a muted nose that opens with swirling, and a balanced, solid wine that surprises him and tastes above its price.

Rose · Video tasting

Casanova Della Spinetta Rose, Toscana

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$ value Italy Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack identifies Casanova Della Spinetta as a Tuscan rose made from Sangiovese and Prugnolo Gentile. He gets fruit, melon, strawberry, a raw-meat note in a good way, gentleness, silkiness, elegance, and the sense that the wine was intentionally made rather than treated as a byproduct.

Cabernet / Bordeaux · Video tasting

Caymus Cabernet Sauvignon, Napa Valley

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$$$ premium USA Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack's captured note is direct: "For the boss gift, Mack heads to the special room and wants name recognition, story, and age-worthiness. He picks Caymus as a Napa Valley legend and frames the $100-plus Cabernet as an impressive gift rather than a casual dinner bottle.". The tone is quick, personal, and more about the moment of the bottle than a formal grid of fruit, acid, and tannin. There is no separate score attached here, so the note has to carry the whole read.

Rank

Champagne / sparkling · Video tasting

Champagne Telmont Reserve de la Terre, Leonardo DiCaprio investment/association

Mack Mack thought captured
👎🏾 Thumbs down from Mack notes $$$$ premium France Bon Appetit Celebrity wine
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack notices Champagne Telmont's modern label and old-style string closure, then gets tiny persistent bubbles, elegance, butterscotch, and honeysuckle. At $90 he wants more acidity, zip, zing, and expression, and says the wine falls flat despite the sustainability story.

Rose · Video tasting

Charles & Charles Rose, Washington State

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$ value USA Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack identifies Charles & Charles as a Washington blend of Syrah, Grenache, Mourvedre, and a little Cabernet Sauvignon. He gets peachy color, fruit, earth, peaches, papaya, guava, fruit cocktail in the best way, texture, grip, tannin, a smoky hint, and says it packs a punch with real flavor.

Other / blend · Retail tasting: Trader Joe's

Charles Shaw Merlot, California 2022

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $ budget USA Bon Appetit Budget pick
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack reads Charles Shaw Merlot as broad California wine with plum, raspberry, black currant, chocolate-covered raspberry, smoke, and a little greenness. The nose has fresh fruit, but the palate feels dull and shut down; for $4.49 he calls it mid, though he has had worse at higher prices.

Cabernet / Bordeaux · Video tasting

Chateau Calon-Segur, Saint-Estephe / Bordeaux

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$ value France Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack's captured note is direct: "For a dinner with skeptical new partner's parents, Mack wants a recognizable region rather than a flashy producer. He chooses Chateau Calon-Segur because Bordeaux carries cellar credibility and the heart on the label fits the relationship story.". The tone is quick, personal, and more about the moment of the bottle than a formal grid of fruit, acid, and tannin. There is no separate score attached here, so the note has to carry the whole read.

Champagne / sparkling · Video tasting

Chateau Deluxe Petillant Naturel, Willamette Valley, Oregon

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$$ standard USA Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack identifies Chateau Deluxe as Willamette Valley pet-nat made from Pinot Noir, Riesling, Gamay, and a little Chardonnay, with a crown cap and trapped primary-fermentation bubbles. He gets Pinot Noir funk, brightness, freshness, high acid, raspberries, natural-wine wildness, and says that for him the only rule is whether the wine is good, and this one is.

Other / blend · Video tasting

Chateau des Tours Brouilly, Beaujolais / France

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$ value France Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack's captured note is direct: "For the post-midnight second bottle, Mack wants a mellow reset after champagne. He chooses Brouilly from Beaujolais for Gamay's crunchy, lively, light-bodied style and calls it affordable Burgundy-adjacent drinking for cruising into the morning.". The tone is quick, personal, and more about the moment of the bottle than a formal grid of fruit, acid, and tannin. There is no separate score attached here, so the note has to carry the whole read.

Rose · Video tasting

Chateau Miraval Rose 2020, Brad Pitt and Angelina Jolie

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$ value France Bon Appetit Celebrity wine
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack places Chateau Miraval in Provence and notes the clay and chalk soils behind the region's rose style. He smells cotton candy, candy floss, strawberry, and kiwi, then finds light-plus body, some weight, decent acidity, and a profile that tastes like Provence rose. He says it is a good example, worth about $24, and rates it with a surfer and smiley face.

Cabernet / Bordeaux · Video tasting

Chop Shop Cabernet Sauvignon Paso Robles 2018

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$ value USA Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack points to Paso Robles and Santa Barbara as useful value regions because land costs are lower. Chop Shop smells dark, rich, currant-like, raspberry-toned, graphite-marked, and less artificially vanilla than the prior Cabernet, though he wants more generosity from the palate.

Pinot Noir · Video tasting

Chosen Family Winery Willamette Valley Pinot Noir 2019, Kevin Love and Channing Frye

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$$ standard USA Bon Appetit Celebrity wine
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack is excited for Chosen Family because he loves Willamette Valley Pinot Noir. He gets Pinot funk, barnyard, mousey notes, tart cherry, raspberry, allspice, fruit plus Old World-like earth, and says the wine is delicious, great, and overdelivers at $35.

Italian red · Video tasting

Ciacci Piccolomini Brunello di Montalcino

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$$ standard Italy Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack calls Ciacci Piccolomini Brunello di Montalcino one of his favorite wines and describes Brunello as rare, DOCG, bigger-skinned Sangiovese, and Italian steak wine: Chianti on steroids. He gets savory iodine, rosemary, dried leather, a hint of cinnamon, cedar, currant, and blackberry, calling it so good and on another level.

Chardonnay · Video tasting

Ciccone Chardonnay 2017, Madonna family

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$$ standard USA Bon Appetit Celebrity wine
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack is wary of Ciccone Chardonnay because Michigan is not known for great wine. He smells canned peas and lime, then finds the Chardonnay very acidic, bland, low in flavor and nuance, and all acid. He explains that Chardonnay is usually richer and rounder, then gives this bottle a thumbs down.

Aromatic / other white · Video tasting

Colli Lapio Fiano di Avellino

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $ budget Italy Bon Appetit Budget pick
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack calls Colli Lapio Fiano di Avellino one of his favorite white wines on the planet and ties it to Campania's volcanic, Mediterranean setting. He gets perfumed florals, white flowers, ripe-but-tart fruit, pear, citrus, stony and flinty gravel-like minerality, and says it feels special, exciting, and one of the great values in white wine.

Mack finds Colonia Las Liebres dark, rich-looking, slightly sulfurous, vegetal, cherry-tomato-like, graphite-marked, and full of raspberry, blackberry, and blueberry-pie fruit. At $9, he calls it surprising, amazing value, and a hidden gem.

Other / blend · Video tasting

Colorado: Brookcliff Vineyards Malbec 2020

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$$ standard Argentina Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack's captured note is direct: "Mack sees the dark Malbec color and gets roasted meat, blackberries, vanilla, and black pepper; he likes the flow, refined feel, and integrated tannins, though the alcohol comes across a little sharp.". The tone is quick, personal, and more about the moment of the bottle than a formal grid of fruit, acid, and tannin. There is no separate score attached here, so the note has to carry the whole read.

Chardonnay · Video tasting

Connecticut: Stonington Seaport White, Chardonnay / Vidal Blanc

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$$ standard Other / mixed regions Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack's captured note is direct: "Mack finds this coastal white fresh, slightly soapy in a pleasant way, with potpourri, green apple, peach pit, lively acidity, structure from Chardonnay, and enough ripeness without overripe fruit.". The tone is quick, personal, and more about the moment of the bottle than a formal grid of fruit, acid, and tannin. There is no separate score attached here, so the note has to carry the whole read.

Syrah / Rhone · Pairing

Cotes du Rhone Syrah

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $ budget France Bon Appetit Budget pick
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack pairs Cotes du Rhone Syrah with an Impossible Whopper because the grilled, smoky burger needs more oomph than a flat-top burger. He gets Lawry's seasoning salt, meatiness, fleshiness, white peppercorn, dried raspberries, good mouthfeel, tannin, and a medium finish, calling it great with regular or plant-based Whoppers.

Daou Rose, Paso Robles, California

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$ value USA Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack identifies Daou as a Paso Robles rose made from 100% Grenache. He gets herbs, bell pepper, very ripe raspberry, a full body, and enough alcohol that it feels a little too much for his idea of rose, which he expects closer to 12% than 14%.

Rose · Video tasting

Dark Horse Rose, California

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$ value USA Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack notes Dark Horse Rose's darker color and explains it could come from thicker-skinned grapes, longer maceration, or blending red and white wine. He gets frosted cherry Pop-Tart, heavy alcohol, and a malt-beverage quality, then says the components are not balanced and he would pass at $10.

Other / blend · Video tasting

Delaware: Salted Vines, exact cuvee not exposed in transcript

Mack Mack thought captured
👎🏾 Thumbs down from Mack notes $$$ standard Other / mixed regions Bon Appetit Pushed back
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack's captured note is direct: "Mack gets potpourri, cotton candy, grape gummies, sweetness, flabby low-acid texture, and a Robitussin-like aftertaste; the aromatics and funk overpower the wine for him.". The tone is quick, personal, and more about the moment of the bottle than a formal grid of fruit, acid, and tannin. There is no separate score attached here, so the note has to carry the whole read.

Cabernet / Bordeaux · Video tasting

Diamond Creek Red Rock Terrace Cabernet Sauvignon, Diamond Mountain, Napa Valley, approximately $300

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$$$$ splurge USA Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack identifies the $300 wine as Diamond Creek Red Rock Terrace Cabernet Sauvignon from the Diamond Mountain AVA and a specific seven-acre vineyard. He says it smells polished and unmistakably Cabernet, with cassis, cinnamon, manure, vanilla, tobacco, truffle, damp earth, and wet dirt. He frames the price as paying for single-vineyard soil, microclimate, reputation, and a place expression that is crystal clear in the glass.

Top notes / pairings Onion, Shallot, Garlic, Pasta, Venison, Tomato-Based, Potato, Pork, Chocolate & Caramel, White Rice
Panel / list rank 👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes
What Others Say

Wine.com aggregates several professional notes for the 2018 Red Rock Terrace, including James Suckling, Connoisseurs' Guide, and Wine Spectator. The shared read is structured Napa Cabernet: dark fruit, mineral/earth detail, polished tannin, and real aging runway.

Other / blend · Video tasting

Dolcetto d'Alba, Skurnik-imported Italian wine

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$$ standard Italy Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack's captured note is direct: "Mack trusts the classic Dolcetto label and the Skurnik importer, expecting tart dark fruit. He finds dust, leather, cedar, raspberries, red berries, richness, ripe fruit, and firm tannin, calling it exactly what Dolcetto should be and an A.". The tone is quick, personal, and more about the moment of the bottle than a formal grid of fruit, acid, and tannin. There is no separate score attached here, so the note has to carry the whole read.

Sauvignon Blanc · Video tasting

Domaine Curry Napa Valley Sauvignon Blanc 2021, Ayesha Curry

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$$ standard USA Bon Appetit Celebrity wine
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack identifies Domaine Curry as 100% Sauvignon Blanc from Calistoga in Napa Valley. He gets grass, a burst of Skittles-like fruit, tropical notes, mango, great acid, weight, body, and less minerality than Bordeaux, then says it is pretty delicious, solid, and still worth considering even at $65.

Other / blend · Video tasting

Domaine Labbe Vin de Savoie "Abymes" 2019

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$ value France Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack places Domaine Labbe Abymes in eastern France and treats the unfamiliar name as part of wine's constant learning curve. He finds chalky minerality, peach, citrus, lemon-lime, electric lemonade brightness, vibrancy, and an oxidative-feeling mineral quality without actual oxidation.

Domaine Le Sang des Cailloux Vacqueyras, Rhone / France

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$$ standard France Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack's captured note is direct: "Mack treats the Vacqueyras label and Kermit Lynch importer as the strongest buying signals in the lineup. He gets dark ruby color, Rhone character, tapenade, black olive, white pepper, savory and slightly bloody notes, a touch of barnyard/Brett character, and calls it leaps and bounds better than the rest: A-plus.". The tone is quick, personal, and more about the moment of the bottle than a formal grid of fruit, acid, and tannin. There is no separate score attached here, so the note has to carry the whole read.

Domaine Skouras Red Blend "Zoe"

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$ value Other / mixed regions Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack closes with Zoe from Greece, built around indigenous Greek grapes with a little Cabernet. He finds earth, mushroom, herbs, rosemary, sage, cherry cola, less obvious fruit, a chillable feel, and a very pleasing palate he would happily drink.

Domitia Picpoul de Pinet 2019

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$ value France Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack uses Domitia Picpoul de Pinet to point toward obscure French white regions that overdeliver. He finds green apple, pear, peach, violet, flinty and slightly smoky minerality, great acid, and calls it a nice little banger that a good wine shop would champion.

Pinot Noir · Video tasting

Enderle & Moll Pinot Noir 2020, Baden, Germany

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$$ standard Other / mixed regions Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack places Enderle & Moll in Baden, Germany's warmest and best Pinot Noir region, then notes the paler raspberry color and lower-sunlight feel. He finds wild, musty, wet-dog, menthol, crushed blackberry, sandstone and limestone minerality, concentrated old-vine fruit, high acid, dill pickle, cooking spice, salinity from the Rhine Valley setting, and calls it a shining German example.

Champagne / sparkling · Retail tasting: Trader Joe's

Ernest Rapeneau Blanc de Blancs Champagne

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $ budget France Bon Appetit Budget pick
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack says Ernest Rapeneau Blanc de Blancs has toasty brioche, pear, flavor depth, layers, and nuance, but has lost its spunk. The bubbles fall off quickly, so it fails to deliver Champagne's dance and excitement; he calls it lackluster and not even good for orange juice.

Syrah / Rhone · Video tasting

Famille Bieler Cotes du Rhone "Return and Reuse"

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$ value France Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack puts Bieler back in the Cotes du Rhone and gets dill, leather, baseball-glove nostalgia, wild strawberry, eucalyptus, mint, acidity, and a long finish. He likes it even more than the previous Rhone bottle and calls it a fuller, more modern take that is straight fire.

Aromatic / other white · Video tasting

Fento Albarino 2020, Rias Baixas / Spain

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $ budget Spain Bon Appetit Budget pick
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack's captured note is direct: "For Feast of the Seven Fishes, Mack wants one coastal white that can handle seafood from start to finish. He chooses Fento Albarino for stone fruit, salinity, lower alcohol, Spanish coastal freshness, and value.". The tone is quick, personal, and more about the moment of the bottle than a formal grid of fruit, acid, and tannin. There is no separate score attached here, so the note has to carry the whole read.

Other / blend · Video tasting

Fergalicious Red Wine Blend, Fergie

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$ value USA Bon Appetit Celebrity wine
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack sees promise in Fergalicious because it names Santa Ynez Valley rather than just California, and he expects a big mature red from the 2016 vintage and 14.4% alcohol. He gets dark color, Cabernet character, medium-plus body, some Old World green edge, a little acetate, and imperfect but energetic authenticity. He calls it a decent $25 bottle that works as a whole.

Figo Prosecco NV

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$ value Italy Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack frames Figo as budget-friendly Italian sparkling wine for celebration. He prefers tasting it from a standard wine glass, then finds lemon-lime, peaches, a hint of mint, ripe fruit, no obvious sweetness, and good acidity.

Fiorini Becco Rosso Lambrusco

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$$ standard Italy Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack presents Fiorini Becco Rosso Lambrusco as a red sparkling wine from Emilia-Romagna and points to secco versus dolce as key label cues. He loves the red fizzy look, gets savory bubblegum and thyme, and says it feels alive: a fun, casual bottle for burger night, pizza night, and tables with lots going on.

Florida: Orange Sunshine, orange wine

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$$ standard Other / mixed regions Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack's captured note is direct: "Mack says the orange wine smells more like orange zest than fresh orange and tastes like a slightly artificial SunnyD; weird but not terrible, better suited to a vodka or gin cocktail than a full glass.". The tone is quick, personal, and more about the moment of the bottle than a formal grid of fruit, acid, and tannin. There is no separate score attached here, so the note has to carry the whole read.

Sauvignon Blanc · Video tasting

Folk Machine B.Y.O.B. California Sauvignon Blanc

Mack Mack thought captured
👎🏾 Thumbs down from Mack notes $ budget USA Bon Appetit Pushed back
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack first finds Folk Machine B.Y.O.B. floral, flabby, low-acid, and likely damaged, then opens a second box. The replacement smells like fruit cocktail and tastes better, but the Sauvignon Blanc character stays muted and he feels something is still missing, so he gives it a disappointed teary-eye reaction.

Cabernet / Bordeaux · Video tasting

Francis Ford Coppola Diamond Collection Cabernet / Claret

Mack Mack thought captured
👎🏾 Thumbs down from Mack notes $$ value USA Bon Appetit Celebrity wine
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack explains Francis Ford Coppola Diamond Collection Claret as a California Cabernet using the English term for Bordeaux-style red wine, then notes the anti-counterfeit-style metal netting. He gets very bright color, raspberry, blueberries, blackberries, leather, cedar box, tannin, and structure, but says something is off. He finds candied underlying sweetness and a manufactured feel, then says he would not pay $21 for it.

Other / blend · Video tasting

Francis Ford Coppola Diamond Collection Merlot

Mack Mack thought captured
👎🏾 Thumbs down from Mack notes $$ value USA Bon Appetit Pushed back
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack starts by defending Merlot and says the Coppola color looks good and the nose smells promising. The palate turns too sweet for him, enough that he jokes it could go over ice cream, and he contrasts it with the Right Bank Bordeaux style of Merlot he would rather drink.

Italian red · Video tasting

Franco Serra Barbera d'Asti 2018

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$ value Italy Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack places Barbera d'Asti in Piedmont and treats it as the local everyday counterpoint to Barolo. He finds it fruity, juicy, ripe, lightly tannic, and the kind of Tuesday-night pizza or hot-dog wine almost anyone could enjoy.

Rose · Video tasting

Franzia Sunset Blush

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $ budget USA Bon Appetit Budget pick
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack approaches Franzia Sunset Blush as the famous party box and gets cotton candy plus slight grassiness. It has almost no acid, sits only on the front palate, feels sweet without reason, and shuts down with no finish; he says it could work for sweet-wine drinkers but gives it a skeptical squiggly-mouth rating.

Chardonnay · Video tasting

Fre Chardonnay, non-alcoholic wine

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$ value Other / mixed regions Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack's captured note is direct: "For the dinner guest who drinks too much, Mack jokes that Fre Chardonnay is the perfect bottle. I logged it as a checked holiday selection, but it is non-alcoholic and should be treated separately from Mack's normal wine evaluations.". The tone is quick, personal, and more about the moment of the bottle than a formal grid of fruit, acid, and tannin. There is no separate score attached here, so the note has to carry the whole read.

Pinot Noir · Video tasting

Fresh Vine Pinot Noir 2018, Nina Dobrev and Julianne Hough

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$ value USA Bon Appetit Celebrity wine
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack approaches Fresh Vine Pinot Noir skeptically because it advertises low carb, low calorie, low sugar, and an active-lifestyle pairing. He says it looks and smells like Pinot Noir, with Earl Grey tea, cherries, strawberry, ripe almost sweet fruit, and some wood treatment. He wants slightly lower alcohol, but says it works as a $19 California Pinot Noir and that drinkers could be happy with it, even if the diet-wine positioning makes him laugh.

Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Rose · Video tasting

Gerard Bertrand Cote des Roses, Languedoc

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$ value France Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack notes the rose-shaped bottle base and glass closure on Gerard Bertrand Cote des Roses before judging the wine past the marketing. He gets spice, white pepper, Syrah, Grenache, and Cinsault character, a little palate sweetness that may be ripe fruit, and says he is surprised enough that he might spend his own money.

Riesling · Video tasting

German Riesling Feinherb 2019

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$ value Other / mixed regions Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

This lands as a holiday-table bottle, the kind you open when the meal and the people matter. Mack's captured note, "For the cooking-all-day Thanksgiving bottle, Mack chooses a lower-alcohol, high-acid German Riesling. He expects pear, stone fruit, lime, and enough freshness to drink while cooking without getting blitzed before dinner.", points less to technical tasting notes and more to occasion. There is no separate score attached here, so the note has to carry the whole read.

Riesling · Pairing

German Riesling Kabinett

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $ budget Other / mixed regions Bon Appetit Budget pick
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack pairs German Riesling Kabinett with Taco Bell because a richer white with low alcohol, acidity, and slight sweetness can handle spicy, meaty Tex-Mex flavors. He gets pears, peaches, lilacs, and a waxy note, calling the pairing classic because it focuses on the protein and keeps the heat in check.

Riesling · Pairing

German Riesling Spatlese

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $ budget Other / mixed regions Bon Appetit Budget pick
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack pairs Riesling Spatlese with the Popeyes spicy chicken sandwich because more sweetness, texture, and low alcohol fight heat without making the spice burn. He gets cotton candy, quince, and kerosene, calls the wine beautiful, and says the pairing could push people to try something unfamiliar with a familiar sandwich.

Chardonnay · Video tasting

GoGi "Goldie" Chardonnay 2018, Kurt Russell

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$$ standard USA Bon Appetit Celebrity wine
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack says GoGi Goldie looks like Chardonnay but smells like a different take on California Chardonnay. Instead of butter and popcorn oak, he gets white-Burgundy-like minerality, rocks, honeysuckle, a big 14.3% frame, and a long finish, calling it well made, flashy, great, iconic, and worth its $50 category.

Sauvignon Blanc · Video tasting

Gordon Ramsay Sauvignon Blanc, Monterey County 2019

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$$ standard USA Bon Appetit Celebrity wine
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack finds lemon-lime, a little gooseberry, and some acid in Gordon Ramsay's Monterey Sauvignon Blanc. He wants it a bit leaner and edgier, but calls it pretty decent, well made for its region, better with food, and good enough to serve in one of Ramsay's restaurants.

Sauvignon Blanc · Video tasting

Graham Norton Sauvignon Blanc, New Zealand 2021

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$ value New Zealand Bon Appetit Celebrity wine
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack says Graham Norton's bottle smells exactly like New Zealand Sauvignon Blanc: gooseberry, citrus, grapefruit, herbaceousness, and canned green bean. He calls it classic, well made, place-true, feel-good, and happy-making, with $18.99 not outrageous even if good examples can be cheaper.

Other / blend · Video tasting

Gulp Hablo Garnacha, Spain

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $ budget Spain Bon Appetit Budget pick
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack's captured note is direct: "Mack likes the back-label clues more than the front label: organic, vegan, T. Edward importer, low alcohol, and a light Grenache color. He gets strawberry and raspberry, calls it easy-drinking and good value, and rates the wine a solid B.". The tone is quick, personal, and more about the moment of the bottle than a formal grid of fruit, acid, and tannin. There is no separate score attached here, so the note has to carry the whole read.

Pinot Noir · Video tasting

Hamilton Russell Pinot Noir, Walker Bay, South Africa

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$$ standard South Africa Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack frames Hamilton Russell as one of South Africa's finest Pinot Noirs and very close to Burgundy in style. He gets pale strawberry color from cool Walker Bay, mineral, earth, sage, thyme, meatiness, savory iron, clay-and-limestone character, concentrated fruit, layered aromatics, mouthfeel, texture, and the evolving qualities Pinot Noir drinkers look for.

Rose · Video tasting

Hampton Water Rose, Jon Bon Jovi, Languedoc

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$ value France Bon Appetit Celebrity wine
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack places Hampton Water in the Languedoc and likes the clean bottle and reusable glass closure for a young, fresh rose. He gets cotton candy, strawberry, citrus peel, minerality, decent acidity, a floral note, and Rhone-variety weight, calling it a good wine though stronger value closer to $17 than $20.

Cabernet / Bordeaux · Video tasting

Harmony Red Bordeaux Blend, Boyz II Men

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$ value France Bon Appetit Celebrity wine
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack identifies Harmony as a Bordeaux red blend and jokes about the vines being played music. He gets slightly mousy character in a good way, pencil shavings, raspberry, blueberries, cassis, great tannins, long finish, iron, and true Bordeaux identity. He calls it great quality, good wine worth your time, a perfect $20 introduction to Bordeaux, and gives it three fire emojis.

Alternative / fruit wine · Video tasting

Hawaii: Maui Blanc, pineapple wine

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$$ standard Other / mixed regions Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack's captured note is direct: "Mack finds real fresh pineapple rather than canned fruit, and is surprised the wine is dry; it reminds him of a tropical Central Coast Chardonnay without much body, but he calls it pleasant, refreshing, well made, and the best fruit wine of the tasting so far.". The tone is quick, personal, and more about the moment of the bottle than a formal grid of fruit, acid, and tannin. There is no separate score attached here, so the note has to carry the whole read.

Cabernet / Bordeaux · Video tasting

Heitz Cellar Cabernet Sauvignon 1978, Napa Valley

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $ budget USA Bon Appetit Budget pick
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack calls the 1978 Heitz phenomenal, with a rich core of fruit, leather, acid, spice, a little anise, lighter body, and a different experience from the younger wines. He says it feels almost like a different grape and excites him because it is like drinking history.

Cabernet / Bordeaux · Video tasting

Heitz Cellar Cabernet Sauvignon 1985, Napa Valley

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $ budget USA Bon Appetit Budget pick
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack finds the 1985 Heitz full of dark Bing cherry, leather, tobacco, cedar, soy-sauce-like fermentation complexity, and old catcher's-mitt/vintage-store character. It is round, not overly tannic, still acidic, still fruity, and in his opinion right at its peak.

Cabernet / Bordeaux · Video tasting

Heitz Cellar Cabernet Sauvignon 1994, Napa Valley

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $ budget USA Bon Appetit Budget pick
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack is excited for the 1994 Heitz as one of Napa's great vintages, but immediately finds it corked. He calls it undrinkable because cork taint gives wet cardboard and wet animal aromas, noting that it is a shame but part of the territory with older bottles.

Cabernet / Bordeaux · Video tasting

Heitz Cellar Cabernet Sauvignon 2007, Napa Valley

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $ budget USA Bon Appetit Budget pick
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack says the 2007 Heitz already smells richer and deeper than the 2016, with layers of ripe but not overripe fruit. It has no drying tannin, still has acidity and fruit, and tastes more velvety as bottle age starts to change the wine's structure.

Cabernet / Bordeaux · Video tasting

Heitz Cellar Cabernet Sauvignon 2016, Napa Valley

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $ budget USA Bon Appetit Budget pick
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack finds the 2016 Heitz Cellar Cabernet youthful and fruit-driven but not over the top, with pretty good acidity and moderate alcohol. He says the acid should help it age well as fruit, acid, alcohol, and tannin become more integrated over time.

Other / blend · Video tasting

Herisson Vin Rouge / Bourgogne Passe-Tout-Grains 2019

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $ budget France Bon Appetit Budget pick
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack reads Herisson as a Burgundy Passe-Tout-Grains blend of Pinot Noir and Gamay, with a vintage and appellation that signal quality. He gets cassis, currant, Earl Grey tea, banana, and bubblegum-like Beaujolais character, then praises its clean, fresh stainless-steel and cement-raised feel as a steal with real terroir.

Other / blend · Video tasting

Hermann J. Wiemer Field White NV

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$ value USA Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack places the Wiemer field blend in the Finger Lakes and notes the Riesling and Chardonnay blend as an odd but intriguing mashup. He sees a neon-like color, more body than expected from Riesling, Chardonnay structure, beautiful aromatics, and calls it a stunner, pleasantly surprising, and out of this world.

Other / blend · Video tasting

Idaho: Ste. Chapelle Open Air Muscat Blanc

Mack Mack thought captured
👎🏾 Thumbs down from Mack notes $$$ standard Other / mixed regions Bon Appetit Pushed back
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack's captured note is direct: "Mack says it smells like Muscat with pear and peach, but the palate is light, strangely jalapeno-like, low in texture and layers, and ultimately flabby without zip or zing.". The tone is quick, personal, and more about the moment of the bottle than a formal grid of fruit, acid, and tannin. There is no separate score attached here, so the note has to carry the whole read.

Illinois: Chambourcin, exact producer not exposed in transcript

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$$ standard Other / mixed regions Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack's captured note is direct: "Mack introduces Chambourcin as a French hybrid, then finds blueberry, raspberry, Kool-Aid-like fruit, black pepper, light body, short finish, little complexity, and not enough concentration or depth.". The tone is quick, personal, and more about the moment of the bottle than a formal grid of fruit, acid, and tannin. There is no separate score attached here, so the note has to carry the whole read.

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$$ standard Other / mixed regions Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack's captured note is direct: "Mack connects Traminette to Gewurztraminer, finding spice, pineapple, nectarine, grass, sweetness, wet stone, and peach; he calls it fun and says the New Alsace, Indiana connection makes the grape choice feel right.". The tone is quick, personal, and more about the moment of the bottle than a formal grid of fruit, acid, and tannin. There is no separate score attached here, so the note has to carry the whole read.

Sauvignon Blanc · Video tasting

Invivo X Sauvignon Blanc, Sarah Jessica Parker, New Zealand

Mack Mack thought captured
👎🏾 Thumbs down from Mack notes $$$ standard New Zealand Bon Appetit Celebrity wine
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack notes Invivo X Sauvignon Blanc is vegan-friendly and then gets asparagus, grapefruit, white pepper, good acidity, and a decent finish. He calls it typical New Zealand Sauvignon Blanc in a good way, but finds it too fruit-forward and wants more minerality and backend detail. He rates it with an anxious teeth emoji because it is missing something needed for a home run.

👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$$ standard Other / mixed regions Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Rose · Video tasting

Isle Saint Pierre Rose, France, magnum

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$ value France Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack uses Isle Saint Pierre's magnum format to explain why large bottles feel festive and can signal quality when the glass and presentation are serious. He gets grass, herbs, melon, raspberry, savory French-rose character, fruit without excess sweetness, terroir, and calls it spot-on value at roughly $10 per bottle inside the magnum.

Aromatic / other white · Video tasting

J. Hofstatter Pinot Grigio

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$$ standard Italy Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack places J. Hofstatter Pinot Grigio in cool Alto Adige and says the region sets a standard for Pinot Grigio with acid and nuance. He gets white flowers, straw, a powdered-sugar aroma without sweetness, acidity, and real palate weight, saying U.S. Pinot Grigio drinkers may like its similar weight while getting a regional Italian expression.

Italian red · Video tasting

Jasci Montepulciano d'Abruzzo

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $ budget Italy Bon Appetit Budget pick
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack introduces Jasci Montepulciano d'Abruzzo as the value-driven kind of Italian wine people mean when they say Europe can overdeliver. He gets cinnamon, vanilla, and cranberry, then calls it a banger with the right acid, the right fruit, mouthwatering food energy, and enough appeal that pronunciation should not keep people from trying it.

Jerome Choblet Muscadet Clos de la Senaigerie

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$ value France Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack calls Muscadet Sur Lie one of his favorite dry French whites and everybody's oyster wine. He gets green apple and a fingernail-polish-remover aroma but not on the palate, then finds acid, mineral, mid-palate texture from lees aging, and says he would not be mad if someone brought it to an oyster party.

Chenin Blanc · Video tasting

Joostenberg "J." Chenin Blanc 2019, Paarl

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$ value South Africa Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack places Joostenberg Chenin Blanc in Paarl, South Africa and calls Chenin Blanc what the cool kids are drinking. He gets ginger, honey, grapefruit, surprisingly full body from the warmer climate, and a style that sits between lean Loire-like Chenin and warmer California examples.

Pinot Noir · Video tasting

Joseph Drouhin Gevrey-Chambertin Pinot Noir 2018, Burgundy, France

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$$ standard France Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack uses Joseph Drouhin Gevrey-Chambertin as the benchmark because Burgundy is Pinot Noir's birthplace. He finds bright ruby color, raspberries, slate, limestone minerality, mushroom-and-soil Old World character, tart cool-climate fruit, high but well-distributed acid, silky-to-satiny texture, sandalwood as it opens, and calls it great.

Other / blend · Video tasting

Joseph Faiveley Bourgogne Rouge 2019

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$ value Other / mixed regions Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

This lands as a holiday-table bottle, the kind you open when the meal and the people matter. Mack's captured note, "For a small family Thanksgiving, Mack wants one versatile bottle and lands on French Pinot Noir. He points to medium body, bright acid, mild fruit, cranberry, earth, and the pleasure of sharing a classic Burgundy producer with family.", points less to technical tasting notes and more to occasion. There is no separate score attached here, so the note has to carry the whole read.

Josh Cellars Rose, California

Mack Mack thought captured
👎🏾 Thumbs down from Mack notes $$ value USA Bon Appetit Pushed back
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack points out that Josh Cellars only gives the broad California appellation, which tells him less about quality than a more specific place would. He finds residual sugar and sweetness that may lure people in, but says the $14 wine leaves him wanting something more compelling.

Other / blend · Video tasting

Kansas: Grace Hill Winery Peckerhead Red

Mack Mack thought captured
👎🏾 Thumbs down from Mack notes $$$ standard Other / mixed regions Bon Appetit Pushed back
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack's captured note is direct: "Mack gets Muscadine-like native-grape perfume from Concord, Chambourcin, and Norton, with potpourri, sweetness, jammy fruit, and wine-cooler energy; he says it is put together but too sweet for him.". The tone is quick, personal, and more about the moment of the bottle than a formal grid of fruit, acid, and tannin. There is no separate score attached here, so the note has to carry the whole read.

Kendall-Jackson Vintner's Reserve Chardonnay 2018

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$ value USA Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack calls Kendall-Jackson a major wine-industry name and finds lots of tropical fruit: pineapple, papaya, green mango, plus luscious vanilla from oak. He says it is not overly buttery but still hits hard in the mouth, and that it works if this richer Chardonnay style is your thing.

Kentucky: Poca Terra American Cabernet Sauvignon

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$$ standard Other / mixed regions Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack's captured note is direct: "Mack sees Cabernet color and cherry-cola aromatics, but finds the wine thin, hot, light-bodied, low in weight, and marked by canned-green-bean character that suggests underripe fruit, young vines, or high yields.". The tone is quick, personal, and more about the moment of the bottle than a formal grid of fruit, acid, and tannin. There is no separate score attached here, so the note has to carry the whole read.

Pinot Noir · Video tasting

Kings Ridge Pinot Noir, Willamette Valley, Oregon

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $ budget USA Bon Appetit Budget pick
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack reads Kings Ridge as entry-level Willamette Pinot that overdelivers. He gets electric raspberry color, raspberry jam, tea, Earl Grey, cherry, mint, ripe raspberry, boysenberry, blackberry, warm baking spice, forest floor, minerality, mushroom, and a best-of-both-worlds mix of New World fruit and Old World terroir.

Champagne / sparkling · Retail tasting: Costco

Kirkland Signature Asolo Prosecco Superiore

Mack Mack thought captured
👎🏾 Thumbs down from Mack notes $ budget Italy Bon Appetit Pushed back
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack finds Kirkland Asolo Prosecco very fragrant, with white flowers, Muscat gummies, citrus, cotton candy, and fruit-cocktail sweetness. He says it is easy to drink or mix if that is your thing, but for his taste it is too sweet and he would look elsewhere for Prosecco.

Cabernet / Bordeaux · Retail tasting: Costco

Kirkland Signature Bordeaux Superieur 2021

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $ budget France Bon Appetit Budget pick
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack says Kirkland Bordeaux Superieur smells like real Bordeaux: graphite, pencil shavings, cassis, raspberry, boysenberry, fruit presence, and dried mushrooms. It falls a little short on the finish, but at $6.89 he says to buy it and thinks it tastes like a bottle that could cost under $30 elsewhere.

Champagne / sparkling · Retail tasting: Costco

Kirkland Signature Brut Champagne

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $ budget France Bon Appetit Budget pick
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack gets toast, brioche, dough, chalk, mineral, marzipan, apple, and stone fruit from Kirkland Brut Champagne, but says it does not really taste like Champagne. The bubbles and mousse fade quickly, leaving him wanting to spend more for something more expressive and terroir-driven.

Cabernet / Bordeaux · Retail tasting: Costco

Kirkland Signature Cabernet Sauvignon, California

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $ budget USA Bon Appetit Budget pick
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack finds raspberry, blueberry, pencil shavings, and a faint hot-oil salon note in Kirkland California Cabernet. It is soft and not consequential, but it actually tastes like wine instead of additives, making it a surprisingly decent everyday intro bottle for its sub-$5-per-750ml math.

Chardonnay · Retail tasting: Costco

Kirkland Signature California Chardonnay, 3-liter box

Mack Mack thought captured
👎🏾 Thumbs down from Mack notes $ budget USA Bon Appetit Pushed back
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack says the Kirkland California Chardonnay box smells like maderized Chardonnay, with reduction, butterscotch, nuttiness, sherry, and no real fruit. It has caramel-apple and oxidized nutty flavors, but he calls oxidation a fault here and says plainly that it is not good and he would pass.

Chardonnay · Retail tasting: Costco

Kirkland Signature Chablis Premier Cru 2021

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$ value France Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack calls Kirkland Chablis Premier Cru serious on paper and a steal if it gets close to real Chablis. In the glass he finds golden color, candy apple, pear, sea-salt air, freshness, decent acidity, caramel apple, wet stone, flint, and gun-smoke minerality, then calls it spot on, tasty, good, and quality Chablis at $18.99.

Other / blend · Retail tasting: Costco

Kirkland Signature Gigondas

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$$ standard France Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack finds iron, blood, cherry, cedar, sandalwood, minerality, and hot-pavement character in Kirkland Gigondas. He calls it delicious, great, full-bodied, powerful, rustic, a shining example of Gigondas, and the most surprising wine of the tasting because it beats a $39 producer bottle he bought over the holidays.

Other / blend · Retail tasting: Costco

Kirkland Signature Malbec, Mendoza, Argentina

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $ budget Argentina Bon Appetit Budget pick
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack finds Kirkland Mendoza Malbec distinctive, with eucalyptus, mint, spearmint, cherry, dark cherry, black cherry, fruit, tannin, structure, herbaceousness, and black pepper spice. It is not the best Malbec he has tasted, but at $6.99 he says you do not go wrong and calls it a big warm hug.

Aromatic / other white · Retail tasting: Costco

Kirkland Signature Pinot Grigio, Friuli, Italy

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $ budget Italy Bon Appetit Budget pick
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack reads Kirkland Pinot Grigio as cool-climate Friuli wine with vibrant fruit, pear, lemon-lime, good acidity, refreshment, and intentional quality. It does not taste watered down, and he calls it a solid, fun, easy-drinking white for $4.99.

Sauvignon Blanc · Retail tasting: Costco

Kirkland Signature Sauvignon Blanc, Marlborough, New Zealand / Ti Point

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $ budget New Zealand Bon Appetit Budget pick
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack gets classic Marlborough Sauvignon Blanc character from the Kirkland/Ti Point bottle: heavy grass, white flowers, perfume, citrus, lemon-lime, bell pepper, green pepper, and a ripe roundness with a little residual sugar. He would like it drier, but calls it great value, unique, distinctive, and fun to drink.

Chardonnay · Retail tasting: Costco

Kirkland Signature Sonoma County Chardonnay

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $ budget USA Bon Appetit Budget pick
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack says Kirkland Sonoma County Chardonnay smells pleasant and oaky, with vanilla, cinnamon, sweetness, roundness, richness, and vanilla-extract flavor. It matches what many people expect from Chardonnay, but the oak character feels chemically induced and out of place to him.

Koha Sauvignon Blanc, Marlborough 2018/2020

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$ value New Zealand Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack uses Koha to introduce New Zealand Sauvignon Blanc and its unmistakable grassy, gooseberry, and cat-pee aromatics. He calls the wine refreshing, bright, grassy, herbaceous, grapefruit-driven, immaculate, and a bottle to try if you want Marlborough Sauvignon Blanc.

Champagne / sparkling · Pairing

Krug Champagne, half bottle

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $ budget France Bon Appetit Budget pick
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack pairs Krug Champagne with a McDonald's Filet-O-Fish dressed up with osetra caviar. He gets toasty fresh-bread character and uses Champagne bubbles to avoid the tinny effect many still whites can have with caviar, calling the result decadent, amazing, celebratory, and over the top.

Other / blend · Video tasting

La Spinetta Moscato d'Asti

Mack Mack thought captured
👎🏾 Thumbs down from Mack notes $$$ standard Italy Bon Appetit Pushed back
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack uses La Spinetta Moscato d'Asti as his first recommendation for people asking about sweet wine. He gets peaches, flowers, muscat-gummy aromatics, apricot, plum, sweetness that is not cloying or syrupy, elegant acidity, and frizzante bubbles that dance on the tongue, then recommends it with fruit-tart-style desserts.

Rose · Video tasting

La Vieille Ferme Rose, France, Grenache / Cinsault / Syrah

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$ value France Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack revisits La Vieille Ferme, a Grenache, Cinsault, and Syrah blend he remembers as an early inexpensive wine. He gets spice that almost makes him sneeze and unexpected sweetness, reading it as made for the American palate and best served cold enough for casual nachos-and-chilling situations.

Other / blend · Video tasting

Las Jaras Glou Glou, Eric Wareheim

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$ value USA Bon Appetit Celebrity wine
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack explains Las Jaras Glou Glou as a clear-bottle red meant to be consumed young and chuggably. He gets dust, leather, old antique store, pomegranate, natural-wine energy that does not overpower place, and calls it fun, cool, drinkable, fairly priced at $28, and his favorite wine of the tasting.

Aromatic / other white · Video tasting

Las Lilas Vinho Verde 2018

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$ value Portugal Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack introduces Las Lilas as Vinho Verde from northern Portugal, a region known for delicious, high-acid exported whites. He expects lightness and freshness, then gets ocean air, clam shells, oyster shells, pear, apple, lemon, lime, and calls it delicious, refreshing, simple in a good way, and easy to pour without overthinking.

Italian red · Video tasting

Le Difese Toscana IGT / Super Tuscan

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$$ standard Italy Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack uses Le Difese Toscana IGT to explain Super Tuscans: Sangiovese blended with noble European grapes like Cabernet or Merlot under looser IGT rules. He gets red currant, black currant, leather, wood, confirmed fruit on the palate, and Sangiovese-driven acidity that gives the wine a modern, innovative mix of Italian and Bordeaux-style worlds.

Les Garrigues Cotes du Rhone

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$ value France Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack places this in the Cotes du Rhone and uses it as a gateway to Rhone grapes like Grenache, Syrah, and Mourvedre. He gets pepper, white pepper, strawberry jam, rosemary, herbs, ripe fruit, and calls it a classic, beautiful expression and a great starting point.

Champagne / sparkling · Video tasting

Licataa Sparkling Lambrusco, Raekwon

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$$ standard Italy Bon Appetit Celebrity wine
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack treats Licataa as Raekwon's sparkling Lambrusco and likes the choice of a chilled sparkling red rather than an obvious Champagne move. He gets wet dirt, cassis, blueberries, raspberries, strawberries, tannin, a dry astringent feel, luxury texture, and staying power. He says the quality and fruit are there and gives it a starry smiley-face rating.

Italian red · Pairing

Light-to-medium Italian red wine, likely Sangiovese/Chianti-style

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $ budget Italy Bon Appetit Budget pick
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack pairs a light-to-medium Italian red, specifically Chianti, with a Subway Italian sub because the tomato, onion, cheese, and salty meats echo classic red-sauce food. He gets tart cherries, raspberries, and oregano, saying the wine acts like seasoning and the affordable classic pairing is hard to lose with.

Pinot Noir · Pairing

Littorai Wendling Vineyard Block E Pinot Noir, Anderson Valley / California

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$$ standard USA instagram.com Pairing picks
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack's captured note is direct: "Opened during a Savannah father-son trip; Mack's caption treats the wine as pure, patient, graceful, and emotionally tied to the road, laughter, and dinner at Husk.". The tone is quick, personal, and more about the moment of the bottle than a formal grid of fruit, acid, and tannin. There is no separate score attached here, so the note has to carry the whole read.

Other / blend · Pairing

Lo-Fi Malbec

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $ budget USA Bon Appetit Budget pick
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack pairs Lo-Fi Malbec with Wendy's chili baked potato because he wants nuance and structure without too much weight. He gets wild cherries, raspberries, smoke, and tobacco, and says its lighter, sophisticated, clean finish complements the rich chili, potato, cheese, and sour cream.

Champagne / sparkling · Video tasting

Louis Roederer Cristal Rose 2008, Champagne

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$$$$ splurge France Bon Appetit Collector / benchmark bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack opens Louis Roederer Cristal Rose 2008 as the $550 prestige bottle, noting the luxury packaging and butterscotch-candy color. He finds very small bubbles, toast, tart apple, strawberries, yeasty breadiness, a long finish, and ripe solidity; he likes it, but says the price puts it beyond most people and whether it is worth it depends on budget.

Roederer's technical material for Cristal 2008 frames the wine around long bottle age, precision, and tension. This is producer material rather than an independent critic review, so it is marked as context, not a sommelier verdict.

Other / blend · Video tasting

Louisiana: Landry Vineyards Blanc du Bois

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$$ standard Other / mixed regions Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack's captured note is direct: "Mack expects sweetness but finds a dry, golden, banana-peel, cardamom, citrus-oil, stony white with oxidative mineral character; it reminds him strongly of Jura wines and excites him as a geeky discovery.". The tone is quick, personal, and more about the moment of the bottle than a formal grid of fruit, acid, and tannin. There is no separate score attached here, so the note has to carry the whole read.

Rose · Video tasting

Luc Belaire Rose, Rick Ross

Mack Mack thought captured
👎🏾 Thumbs down from Mack notes $$$ standard France Bon Appetit Celebrity wine
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack notes that Luc Belaire is French sparkling rose but not Champagne, and dismisses the 'real French rose' marketing tag. He gets raspberry, strawberry, grapefruit, white pepper, deep Shiraz-driven color, heavy carbonation, and a slightly sweet profile that could work with cheese, but on its own feels overpriced and not pleasurable to him.

Champagne / sparkling · Video tasting

LVE Sparkling Blanc de Blancs 2016, John Legend

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$$ standard USA Bon Appetit Celebrity wine
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack identifies John Legend's LVE as a 2016 sparkling Blanc de Blancs from Napa Valley, made from Chardonnay. He gets brioche, creme brulee, tremendous texture, a velvety palate, soft mousse, and mellow bottle maturity, saying the sophisticated, mature, sultry feel matches John Legend's image.

Alternative / fruit wine · Video tasting

Maine: Dragonfly Farm and Winery Apple Table Wine

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$$ standard Other / mixed regions Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack's captured note is direct: "Mack explains this as apple wine rather than cider, then finds little apple, a faint pear-like fruit note, strong fingernail-polish-remover aroma, and a nutty finish; he does not like this one.". The tone is quick, personal, and more about the moment of the bottle than a formal grid of fruit, acid, and tannin. There is no separate score attached here, so the note has to carry the whole read.
