Sauvignon Blanc · Video tasting

Maison Cubi Sauvignon Blanc, France 2020

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$$ standard France Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack says Maison Cubi looks and smells like Sauvignon Blanc, with grassy, herbaceous aromatics and a little grapefruit. It has some acid and tastes French, but it also tastes off, short on fruit and minerality, and priced too high at $35 compared with where he thinks the value should land.

Rose · Video tasting

Maison No. 9 Rose, Post Malone

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$ value France Bon Appetit Celebrity wine
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack notes Maison No. 9's fluted bottle, glass cap, and Post Malone sword detail before tasting. He finds great clear color, cotton candy, strawberry, canned fruit cocktail, structure, balance, and good acid. He is surprised by the quality and calls it a great-value $25 rose that hits the notes it should.

Cabernet / Bordeaux · Video tasting

Mayacamas Vineyards Cabernet Sauvignon, Mount Veeder, Napa Valley, approximately $135

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$$$ premium USA Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack identifies the $135 bottle as Mayacamas Vineyard Cabernet Sauvignon from Mount Veeder, a more specific Napa location. He gets intense currant, graphite, lead, and pencil shavings, calling it pretty amazing. He uses the bottle to show how the wines become more expressive with price and place detail: rosemary, sage, graphite, pencil, currant, and cassis become clearer.

Rose · Video tasting

McBride Sisters Black Girl Magic Rose, California

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$ value USA Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack describes Black Girl Magic Rose as neon raspberry in color. He gets strawberry, cotton candy, citrus, and a solid overall profile, though he wants more acidity and more appellation detail inside California for a $19 bottle.

Sauvignon Blanc · Video tasting

McFadden Vineyards "Blue Quail" Sauvignon Blanc, Potter Valley 2018/2019

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$ value USA Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack identifies Blue Quail as California Sauvignon Blanc from Mendocino County. He finds herbaceous green pepper, a little cantaloupe, crispness, great acid that stays in balance, and some minerality, calling the wine pretty perfect for the style.

Sauvignon Blanc · Video tasting

Michel Girard Sancerre, Loire Valley / France

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$ value France Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack's captured note is direct: "For coming back after Dry January, Mack wants to ease in with Sauvignon Blanc from Sancerre. He points to grassiness, herbaceousness, minerality, acid, lower alcohol, and a classic-looking label while advising shoppers to ask the wine shop for producer help.". The tone is quick, personal, and more about the moment of the bottle than a formal grid of fruit, acid, and tannin. There is no separate score attached here, so the note has to carry the whole read.

Champagne / sparkling · Video tasting

Michel Gonet Blanc de Blancs Champagne 2012

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$$ standard France Bon Appetit Collector / benchmark bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack presents Michel Gonet 2012 as grower Champagne made entirely from Chardonnay and immediately expects quality because Champagne is on the label. He finds tiny bubbles, neon green-yellow color, brioche, white flowers, apple, Bosc pear, caramelized bottle maturity, mineral, and flint, then calls it one of the best reasonable-price Champagne examples and his favorite of the tasting.

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$$ standard USA Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack's captured note is direct: "Mack says the Vidal Blanc ice wine smells amazing, with stewed peach, pear, and mirabelle plum; he loves the texture, richness, layers, and dessert-wine soul, and calls it pretty tasty and worth trying.". The tone is quick, personal, and more about the moment of the bottle than a formal grid of fruit, acid, and tannin. There is no separate score attached here, so the note has to carry the whole read.

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$$ standard Other / mixed regions Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack's captured note is direct: "Mack finds funk, golden color, power, tannin, white pepper, and rough edges; the wine feels hearty but unpolished, unfamiliar, one-dimensional, alcoholic, heavy, and not enjoyable to him.". The tone is quick, personal, and more about the moment of the bottle than a formal grid of fruit, acid, and tannin. There is no separate score attached here, so the note has to carry the whole read.

Mississippi: R&R Vineyards Lomanto

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$$ standard Other / mixed regions Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack's captured note is direct: "Mack learns Lomanto is the grape, then gets dark purple color, native-grape musk, Syrah-like spice, tannin, and fruit that drops out on the palate; he sees it as incomplete but part of a growing Anything But Vinifera movement.". The tone is quick, personal, and more about the moment of the bottle than a formal grid of fruit, acid, and tannin. There is no separate score attached here, so the note has to carry the whole read.

Other / blend · Video tasting

Montana: Hidden Legend Banned in France 2018 red wine

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$$ standard France Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack finds cinnamon, nutmeg, baking spice, raw meat, and herbaceousness in Hidden Legend Banned in France. It falls short on the palate and is not all that exciting, but he likes the lack of foxy hybrid character and says he is intrigued even if he would not want a whole bottle.

Pinot Noir · Video tasting

Moorooduc Estate Pinot Noir 2018, Mornington Peninsula, Australia

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$$ standard Australia Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack treats Moorooduc Estate as a more exciting Australian counterpoint to Shiraz. He notes darker color with a little orange from bottle maturity, then gets minerality, cedar, sandalwood, confectionary cotton-candy-like ripeness without sweetness, meaty soil character, a little kerosene, New World fruit, integrated acid, aromatics, savory detail, and a balanced maritime-climate expression.

Champagne / sparkling · Video tasting

Mouson-Leroux Rose Champagne / Rose de Saignee

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$$$ premium France Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack describes Mouson-Leroux as a 100% Pinot Noir rose de saignee Champagne with very small, persistent bubbles. He gets forest floor, leaves, damp earth, meat-tenderizer savoriness, candied apple without sweetness, white peach, nectarine, and slight grassiness, then says the grand cru fruit makes the $90 price make sense and maybe even look low.

Other / blend · Video tasting

MYX Fusions Peach Moscato, Nicki Minaj

Mack Mack thought captured
👎🏾 Thumbs down from Mack notes $$$ standard USA Bon Appetit Celebrity wine
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack reads MYX Fusions as Peach Moscato blended with natural fruit juices and notes that it is kosher. He smells strong peach, slight sulfur like a hair salon, and vanilla, then finds sweetness without much acid and a one-dimensional profile. Compared with Moscato d'Asti, he says it lacks refinement, bubbles, elegance, and layers.

Other / blend · Video tasting

Natural Origins Organic Malbec, Mendoza, Argentina

Mack Mack thought captured
👎🏾 Thumbs down from Mack notes $ budget Argentina Bon Appetit Pushed back
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack notes Natural Origins is a vegan-friendly organic Malbec from Mendoza with a 2019 vintage, but the neon color makes him wary. He gets pepper, leather, plum, and currant, then says the wine tastes fake-ish and too manipulated, lacking the earth and nuance he wants from Malbec.

Other / blend · Video tasting

Nebraska: Miletta Vista Edelweiss

Mack Mack thought captured
👎🏾 Thumbs down from Mack notes $$$ standard Other / mixed regions Bon Appetit Pushed back
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack reads Miletta Vista Edelweiss as very perfumed, aromatic, peachy, apricot-like, and sweet. The problem is no acid: it feels flabby, not lively, and reflective of a young regional wine industry that still has room to improve.

Other / blend · Video tasting

Nevada: Battle Born Semillon, Artisan Sellers

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$$ standard Other / mixed regions Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack notes that Semillon is famous in Bordeaux, then finds the Nevada version chalky, dried-grape-like, tannic, drying, big, hot, and high in alcohol for a white wine. He says it may suit someone looking for big and bold, but to him the alcohol is over the top and not enjoyable.

Other / blend · Video tasting

New Hampshire: Shara Vineyards Equinox 2019, Marquette

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$$ standard Other / mixed regions Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack identifies Shara Equinox as Marquette, a native American hybrid, and gets violets, raspberries, currant, white pepper, and blackberry. The tannin dominates, drying his mouth out, while the texture and richness fall away compared with something like Tannat.

Other / blend · Video tasting

New Jersey: Palmaris Merlot 2018

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$$ standard Other / mixed regions Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack says Palmaris Merlot smells like Merlot, with pencil shavings, graphite, plum, gravel, and dirt road. It is a little light, but interesting, well made, Bordeaux-leaning for the coastal climate, and good enough that he says it all adds up and tastes great.

Other / blend · Video tasting

New Mexico: Rio Grande Winery Mission 2019

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$$ standard Other / mixed regions Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack sees a pink-orange salmon color in Rio Grande Mission and smells bubble gum, dried apricots, and diesel fuel. He likes the larger Mission-grape movement and its history, but this bottle tastes older than 2019, oxidized, short on fruit, and maybe not one of the better examples.

Riesling · Video tasting

New York: Hermann J. Wiemer Flower Day Riesling

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$$ standard USA Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack treats Hermann J. Wiemer Flower Day Riesling as a standard-setter for New York wine. He gets classic petrol, kerosene, apricots, apples, and white flowers, with sweetness lifted by acid so the wine feels alive, fresh, and like a warm hug.

Chardonnay · Video tasting

No Es Pituko Chardonnay, Chile

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $ budget Chile Bon Appetit Budget pick
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

This lands as a holiday-table bottle, the kind you open when the meal and the people matter. Mack's captured note, "For vegetarian Thanksgiving, Mack leans natural because the scenario values environment and health. He calls No Es Pituko a medium-bodied, full-flavored Chilean Chardonnay with decent acidity, natural-wine texture, and visible crystals from the unfined/unfiltered approach.", points less to technical tasting notes and more to occasion. There is no separate score attached here, so the note has to carry the whole read.

North Carolina: Dublin Vineyards Scuppernong Blush

Mack Mack thought captured
👎🏾 Thumbs down from Mack notes $$$ standard Other / mixed regions Bon Appetit Pushed back
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack reacts strongly against Dublin Vineyards Scuppernong Blush, calling the aroma weird and the taste really sweet, with sweet bubble gum and witch hazel. He places it in line with other Muscadine wines he has tasted and says it is just not his thing.

North Dakota: Dakota Vines Roughrider Red 2021, American Frontenac

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$$ standard Other / mixed regions Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack finds Dakota Vines Roughrider Red a little sulfury in a familiar hot-oil-salon way, with blackberry and currant. The sweetness makes it taste like a corner-store malt beverage, with little structure, and he says it could be much better.

Pinot Noir · Video tasting

Ohio: Debonne Pinot Noir, Grand River Valley

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$$ standard Other / mixed regions Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack says Debonne Pinot Noir smells and tastes like Pinot Noir, showing funk, cherry, mushroom, earth, tannin, and acid. It leads with terroir and earth more than fruit, has a slight green vegetal edge, but he calls it a great local Ohio example he would be proud of.

Other / blend · Video tasting

Oklahoma: Pecan Creek Winery Vignoles 2021

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$$ standard Other / mixed regions Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack finds Pecan Creek Vignoles foxy right away, with cotton candy, strong acidity, heavy citrus rind, and grassy notes. He is unfamiliar with the hybrid grape but intrigued enough that he could sip through the bottle trying to understand it.

Alternative / fruit wine · Pairing

Orange wine

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $ budget Other / mixed regions Bon Appetit Budget pick
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack pairs dry orange wine with Panda Express orange chicken because the skin-contact white can handle sweetness and heat. He gets tangerine, ginger, lychee, lean texture, low acid, and bone dryness, and enjoys the literal orange-wine-with-orange-chicken move.

Pinot Noir · Video tasting

Oregon: Holloran Pinot Noir 2016, Willamette Valley / Dundee Hills

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$$ standard USA Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack calls Holloran Pinot Noir beautiful, with Earl Grey tea, high-toned red cherry, grip without excessive tannin, and waves of refreshing acidity. He uses it to frame Oregon and the Willamette Valley as a best-of-both-worlds bridge between New World fruit and Old World sense of place.

Italian red · Video tasting

Paolo Conterno Barolo

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$$ standard Italy Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack frames Paolo Conterno Barolo as the king of Italian wines, built from Nebbiolo with serious aging requirements. He gets floral rose petals, asphalt, dried cranberry, a hint of strawberry, and intense tannin that dries the whole mouth, calling it beautiful, special, and unlike almost anything else in wine.

Italian red · Video tasting

Paolo Scavino Barbera d'Alba 2022

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$$ standard Italy Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack introduces Paolo Scavino Barbera d'Alba as everyday Piedmont drinking wine and everyday luxury. He gets raspberry and plum-pie fruit, easy drinkability, acidity without heavy jamminess, and a food-friendly salivating quality, calling it a great starting point and training-wheels entry into Italian wine for New World drinkers.

Other / blend · Video tasting

Paolo Scavino Langhe Bianco "Sorriso" 2019

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$ value Italy Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack identifies Sorriso as a Langhe blend of Chardonnay, Sauvignon Blanc, and Viognier. He gets herbaceous grassy notes, violets, florals, rich mouthfeel, and Chardonnay structure, then calls it top notch, incredible, and his number-one white wine of the tasting because it has texture, body, and soul beyond its price.

Rose · Video tasting

Pasaeli Rose, Turkey, Yapincak/Yugen grape as transcribed

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$ value Other / mixed regions Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack introduces Pasaeli as a Turkish rose from a local grape transcribed as Yugen. He gets citrus, strawberry, raspberry, and real body with roundness rather than overpowering weight, then says exploring Turkey is part of what makes wine fun and that he would drink this all day.

Aromatic / other white · Pairing

Pazo Barrantes "La Comtesse" 2019 Albarino, Rias Baixas / Spain

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$$ standard Spain instagram.com Pairing picks
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack's captured note is direct: "Used as the elevated wine pairing for an Italian tuna and prosciutto sandwich; the caption frames it as textured, elegant, expressive, and a major step up for Albarino.". The tone is quick, personal, and more about the moment of the bottle than a formal grid of fruit, acid, and tannin. There is no separate score attached here, so the note has to carry the whole read.

Pennsylvania: Stony Run Riesling 2017, Lehigh Valley

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$$ standard Other / mixed regions Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack notes that Stony Run Riesling is labeled dry and a little older for white wine. He gets petrol, funk, faint baby powder that fades, bone-dry structure, great acid, and a Germanic Riesling influence that he finds pretty impressive.

Champagne / sparkling · Pairing

Petillant sparkling wine

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $ budget Other / mixed regions Bon Appetit Budget pick
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack pairs petillant sparkling red with KFC because light bubbles scrub the palate and the red fruit echoes the chicken seasoning. He gets earthy mud-bath tones, cranberry, cherry, and lots of strawberry, calling petillant fun, accessible, and well matched to salty fried chicken skin.

Champagne / sparkling · Video tasting

Philipponnat Clos des Goisses 2011, Champagne

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$$$$ splurge France Bon Appetit Collector / benchmark bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack treats Philipponnat Clos des Goisses 2011 as a $375 tete de cuvee made from 100% Pinot Noir. He sees golden straw color darkening with age and gets marzipan, yeast, refinement, smoke, evolving complexity, tiny bubbles, stature, charisma, and all the qualities he wants in Champagne, saying it really does taste like a top cuvee.

Cabernet / Bordeaux · Video tasting

Point Ormond Cabernet Sauvignon

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$ value Australia Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack uses Point Ormond to explain Cabernet Sauvignon as a thick-skinned noble grape that makes broad, burly wines in warm places like Australia. In the glass he gets boysenberry, pencil shavings, raspberry, currant, and a big fruit-bomb profile that he wants with barbecue ribs.

Champagne / sparkling · Video tasting

Pol Roger Champagne, magnum

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $ budget France Bon Appetit Budget pick
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack's captured note is direct: "For the first half of an all-night New Year's Eve party, Mack wants a magnum because it looks festive, ages well, and turns champagne into a shared party object. He calls Pol Roger classic champagne and says the large format represents value when doubled from a normal bottle.". The tone is quick, personal, and more about the moment of the bottle than a formal grid of fruit, acid, and tannin. There is no separate score attached here, so the note has to carry the whole read.

Porto Kopke Fine Ruby Port

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$ value Other / mixed regions Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack's captured note is direct: "For an ugly sweater party with desserts and snacks, Mack reaches for sweet fortified wine. He chooses Kopke Ruby Port for leather, plums, cassis, currant, a coating mouthfeel, and a long finish that can stand up to dessert.". The tone is quick, personal, and more about the moment of the bottle than a formal grid of fruit, acid, and tannin. There is no separate score attached here, so the note has to carry the whole read.

Pursued by Bear Cabernet Sauvignon 2018, Kyle MacLachlan

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$$ standard USA Bon Appetit Celebrity wine
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack places Pursued by Bear in Washington's Columbia Valley with Walla Walla context and goes in excited for Cabernet. He finds crazy raspberry, confectionary sweetness, cedar, leather, graphite, a big finish, cinnamon, nuance, layers, balanced alcohol, and calls it serious wine and a great Washington Cabernet showing at $70.

Champagne / sparkling · Video tasting

Ridgeview Blanc de Blancs, England

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$$ standard Other / mixed regions Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack identifies Ridgeview as English blanc de blancs from 100% Chardonnay and finds lovely mousse, yellow straw color, brioche, caramelized apples, dryness, strong acid, and minerality. He explains that England's cool climate, single-vineyard sourcing, 2014 vintage, traditional method, and hand work support the price, and from tasting it he understands why it is celebrated there.

Other / blend · Video tasting

Sandy Giovese Vino Rosso, Italy

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $ budget Italy Bon Appetit Budget pick
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack likes Sandy Giovese's chill-me cue and finds earth, dirt, strawberries, violets, and cherries. He says it tastes like real wine: lightweight, juicy, acidic, quietly complex, and such a knockout that he would aspire to make boxed wine like it; it ends as his favorite wine of the day.

Mack's captured note is direct: "For January 1 recovery brunch, Mack prioritizes mimosas over prestige. The tone is quick, personal, and more about the moment of the bottle than a formal grid of fruit, acid, and tannin. There is no separate score attached here, so the note has to carry the whole read.

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$ value Austria Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack names Gobelsburg as an Austrian blend of Zweigelt, St. Laurent, and Pinot Noir. He finds zip, pop, beautiful balanced acidity, and the kind of real, intentional wine that begs to be drunk now anywhere, not just by the pool in summer.

Schloss Lieser Estate Riesling "SL" Feinherb 2016, Thomas Haag

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$ value Other / mixed regions Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack uses Schloss Lieser Riesling to explain Germany's fluted bottles and off-dry Riesling style. He gets classic petrol/kerosene, unripe pear, green apple, lemon zest, residual sugar balanced by strong acidity, and says the wine finishes dry enough to make Riesling and French fries a joyful pairing.

Aromatic / other white · Video tasting

Schplink Gruner Veltliner 2019, Austria

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$$ standard Austria Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack is encouraged by Schplink Gruner Veltliner's Austrian origin and 2019 vintage, then gets red apple, spearmint, strong acidity, minerality, and a salty edge. He calls it textbook Gruner Veltliner: racy, mineral-driven, asparagus-friendly, high-quality for the boxed price, and evidence that boxed wines in the $30 range can level up.

Schramsberg Blanc de Noirs 2017, California

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$$ standard USA Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack calls Schramsberg Blanc de Noirs one of his favorite domestic sparkling wines. He sees a beautiful salmon-pink color and gets golden apple, apricot, brioche, toast, mango, pineapple, dryness, and a long finish, saying he could call it Champagne blind and that California making sparkling wine at this level is a feat worth experiencing.

Aromatic / other white · Video tasting

Setzer Gruner Veltliner 2019

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$ value Austria Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack closes in Austria with Setzer Gruner Veltliner, explaining its sommelier popularity and famous usefulness with asparagus. He finds dry white-wine freshness, great acid, a beautiful refreshing profile, and calls it spot-on textbook Gruner Veltliner.

Cabernet / Bordeaux · Video tasting

Sidekick Cabernet Sauvignon, California, approximately $18

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$ value USA Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack starts the price ladder with the $18 California Cabernet, later naming it Sidekick. He notes that the back label only says California, then gets red berries, currant, red fruit, black fruit, and a hint of vanilla likely from oak. He calls it pretty good, really great, and well made, while saying it lacks the nuanced place-specific details that appear as the wines get more expensive.

Champagne / sparkling · Video tasting

Soliera Lambrusco, Italy

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$ value Italy Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack's captured note is direct: "For the white elephant gift swap, Mack wants a sub-$20 bottle that feels different enough to fight over. He chooses a sparkling red Lambrusco, notes the lower alcohol may signal sweetness, recognizes the importer, and says the bubbles, red color, and festive look would grab attention.". The tone is quick, personal, and more about the moment of the bottle than a formal grid of fruit, acid, and tannin. There is no separate score attached here, so the note has to carry the whole read.

Alternative / fruit wine · Video tasting

South Carolina: Low Country Red, Muscadine / Ison

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$$ standard Other / mixed regions Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack goes in hoping Low Country Red might change his mind about Muscadine, but says it is not the one. It smells like aromatic, herbaceous Amaro and tastes like Amaro without the high alcohol; as wine it is not palatable to him, though he can imagine it working in a cocktail.

Cabernet / Bordeaux · Video tasting

Steady State Cabernet Sauvignon, Napa Valley, approximately $59

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$$ standard USA Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack moves from generic California into the $59 Napa Valley Cabernet, later identifying it as Steady State. He explains that Napa fruit carries a price because of the region's microclimates and soils, then gets herbs with more specificity: rosemary, sage, lavender, cassis, and a little lead. He says these subtle nuances help explain the jump from the $18 bottle.

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$ value Italy Bon Appetit Episode bottles

Mack places Masera in Gavi and uses the screwcap to talk about Stelvin closures. In the glass he gets white flowers, green apple, citrus, and a beautiful, delightful, light white-wine style that can move Pinot Grigio drinkers into more interesting coastal Italian whites.

Summer Water Rose, California

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$ value USA Bon Appetit Episode bottles

Mack reads Summer Water as a salmon-pink California rose and immediately gets prosciutto and melon. He says the wine has enough alcohol weight to avoid feeling flimsy, likes it, and thinks the $18 bottle earns its extra price jump.

Aromatic / other white · Video tasting

Sun Goddess Ramato Pinot Grigio, Mary J. Blige

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$ value Italy Bon Appetit Celebrity wine
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack explains Sun Goddess as ramato Pinot Grigio, where skin contact gives the wine its copper-pink color and more texture. He gets perfume, flower bouquet, white flowers, peach, nectarine, unexpected cantaloupe, a kiss of backend sweetness, and decent acid. He calls it interesting, authentic-feeling, and great quality for $25, giving it a loudspeaker emoji because he wants people to know about it.

Alternative / fruit wine · Video tasting

Tennessee: Tsali Notch Vineyards White Muscadine

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$$ standard Other / mixed regions Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack finds Tsali Notch White Muscadine wild, foxy, fruity, sweet, textured, and lightly acidic. He sees a higher quality level than some other Muscadine wines, but the style still is not to his taste and he personally would not serve it, though sweet-wine drinkers might like it.

Italian red · Video tasting

Tenuta delle Terre Nere Etna Rosso

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$$ standard Italy Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack places Tenuta delle Terre Nere Etna Rosso on the volcanic soils of Mount Etna and focuses on the mineral character that comes from that setting. He finds beautiful color, rich aromatics, hearty pepper, refined white pepper, bright currant and cherry fruit without sweetness, metallic minerality, and says the wine makes him want to drink more Etna Rosso.

Italian red · Video tasting

Tenuta Le Calcinaie Chianti Colli Senesi

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$$ standard Italy Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack identifies Tenuta Le Calcinaie Chianti Colli Senesi as 100% Sangiovese and uses it to explain Chianti's dusty, less-fruit-led character. He gets raspberries, blackberry, leather, dust, a rough but correct Chianti-style finish, body, structure, and enough framework to read as very much Chianti across the board.

Other / blend · Video tasting

Texas: Pedernales Cellars Tempranillo 2019

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$$ standard USA Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack gets warm baking spices and a little hair-salon sulfur from Pedernales Tempranillo, which checks out for the grape to him. Compared with Rioja, this Texas version is lusher, bigger, rounder, higher in alcohol, clearly American, and one he likes.

Syrah / Rhone · Video tasting

Thalvin / Alain Graillot Syrocco Syrah, Morocco

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$$ standard Other / mixed regions Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack's captured note is direct: "Mack is intrigued by Morocco, the Syrah grape, and the producer partnership. In the glass he gets dark Syrah color, meat, savory herbs, rosemary, and everything he wants Syrah to be, raising it to an A.". The tone is quick, personal, and more about the moment of the bottle than a formal grid of fruit, acid, and tannin. There is no separate score attached here, so the note has to carry the whole read.

Rose · Video tasting

The Beach by Whispering Angel Rose, Provence

Mack Mack thought captured
👎🏾 Thumbs down from Mack notes $$ value France Bon Appetit Pushed back
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack notes the colorful, inviting packaging on The Beach by Whispering Angel and then tastes past the label. He smells rice pudding and cinnamon, gets watermelon and some smoke on the palate, and calls it not bad but one-dimensional because the nose does not carry through to the finish.

Pinot Noir · Video tasting

The Calling Russian River Pinot Noir, Fox Den Vineyard, Jim Nantz

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$$ standard USA Bon Appetit Celebrity wine
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack notes The Calling's heavy bottle and distinctive metal-like label, then tastes Fox Den Vineyard Pinot Noir from Russian River. He gets dried cranberry, cherry, black tea, spice, nutmeg, well-integrated alcohol, classic Russian River character, and calls the wine delicious and tasty from one of his favorite Pinot Noir regions.

Champagne / sparkling · Video tasting

The Chook Sparkling Shiraz, South Australia

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$$ standard Australia Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack introduces The Chook as a fun South Australian sparkling Shiraz, a style many people do not realize exists. He gets blackberry, blueberry, cocoa nib, richness, roundness, and plenty of red-wine tannin, then uses the bottle to explain traditional-method sparkling wine and why the labor usually affects price.

Cabernet / Bordeaux · Video tasting

The Dreaming Tree Cabernet Sauvignon, Dave Matthews

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$ value USA Bon Appetit Celebrity wine
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack identifies The Dreaming Tree as a California Cabernet and gets boysenberries, blackberries, red currants, and a tiny bit of cedar without much vanilla or new-oak signature. He finds decent tannins and finish but wants more body, structure, roundness, richness, and texture from Cabernet. For $15 he is not mad at it and thinks many drinkers would be happy.

The Little Sheep of France Rose

Mack Mack thought captured
👎🏾 Thumbs down from Mack notes $$ value France Bon Appetit Pushed back
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack finds The Little Sheep of France one-dimensional and sweet, especially given that it is a French rose. He says it tastes like several other wines in the lineup, does not thrill him, lacks a clear sense of place, and that shoppers could probably do better for less than $12.

The Pinot Project Rose, Pinot Noir, France

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$ value France Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack treats The Pinot Project as an unusual single-varietal Pinot Noir rose at a $10 price point. He gets Lucky Charms marshmallows, cantaloupe, and raspberry, finds it neither overly sweet nor tart, and says it checks the box of tasting like something real from the earth.

Syrah / Rhone · Retail tasting: Trader Joe's

Trader Joe's Grand Reserve GSM, Grenache / Syrah / Mourvedre

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $ budget USA Bon Appetit Budget pick
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack uses the Grand Reserve GSM to explain Grenache, Syrah, and Mourvedre as Rhone grapes, then gets iron, metallic savoriness, blood-like Mourvedre character, pepper, cranberry, currant, tannin, and high alcohol. He wanted more lush fruit and layers and says better Cotes du Rhone can be found for the same or less money.

Other / blend · Retail tasting: Trader Joe's

Trader Joe's Grand Reserve Meritage, Paso Robles 2021

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $ budget USA Bon Appetit Budget pick
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack gets pronounced ripe Paso fruit from Trader Joe's Grand Reserve Meritage: ripe plum, red currant, cocoa, vanilla, and cherry. He likes it because the glass delivers what the label advertises: rich, ripe, bountiful fruit and a clear Bordeaux-style blend promise.

Other / blend · Retail tasting: Trader Joe's

Trader Joe's Grower's Reserve Red Wine Blend 2023

Mack Mack thought captured
👎🏾 Thumbs down from Mack notes $ budget USA Bon Appetit Pushed back
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack finds violets, raspberry, vanilla, cinnamon, and a chemically induced quality in Trader Joe's Grower's Reserve Red Wine Blend. Despite its organic label, it tastes manipulated to him, with fingernail-polish-remover notes, and he says he would not drink it.

Sauvignon Blanc · Retail tasting: Trader Joe's

Trader Joe's Napa Valley Sauvignon Blanc, exact tier not exposed in transcript

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $ budget USA Bon Appetit Budget pick
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack says Trader Joe's Reserve Sauvignon Blanc smells faintly of cotton candy, grass, herbs, salt, and ocean air. On the palate it has great acidity, creamy texture, lemon, and lemon verbena, and he calls it spot on, solid, true to Napa Sauvignon Blanc, and a bargain at $9.99.

Other / blend · Retail tasting: Trader Joe's

Trader Joe's Petit Reserve Carmenere, Chile 2021

Mack Mack thought captured
👎🏾 Thumbs down from Mack notes $ budget Chile Bon Appetit Pushed back
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack gets bell pepper, green pepper, cranberry, cinnamon, black currant, ripe fruit, impact, flavor, and richness from Trader Joe's Petit Reserve Carmenere. It lacks a little acidity, but at $7.99 he calls it a fun pizza-and-movie bottle to explore without much financial risk.

Trader Joe's Pinot Gris, Willamette Valley, Oregon 2023

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $ budget USA Bon Appetit Budget pick
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack finds lemon-lime, lemon custard, cotton candy, peach, nectarine, high acidity, and a touch of residual sugar in Trader Joe's Willamette Pinot Gris. He calls it pretty good, great at $9.99, and a winner because the Willamette Valley designation already signals more quality.

Cabernet / Bordeaux · Retail tasting: Trader Joe's

Trader Joe's Platinum Reserve Cabernet Sauvignon, Oakville / Napa Valley

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$ value USA Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack says the Platinum Reserve Oakville Cabernet smells like high-quality Napa Cabernet: cedar, leather, black currant, red currant, bitter chocolate, and integrated tannin. It is polished, balanced, mature, and an incredible $17.99 value, though it falls short on the finish.

Pinot Noir · Retail tasting: Trader Joe's

Trader Joe's Reserve Pinot Noir, Santa Barbara County

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $ budget USA Bon Appetit Budget pick
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack gets Pinot funk, cherry, raspberry, and dried dirt from Trader Joe's Reserve Pinot Noir. The alcohol dominates so much that he calls it somewhat undrinkable, too hot, and short on fruit, recommending a hard chill just to tame the burn.

Trader Joe's Reserve Rose of Pinot Noir 2023

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $ budget USA Bon Appetit Budget pick
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack finds Pinot funk, raspberry, kiwi, strawberry, Muscat-gummy aromatics, mowed-lawn freshness, and smoke in Trader Joe's Reserve Rose of Pinot Noir. He calls it juicy, fun, fragrant, aromatic, clearly detailed by its Carneros source, and a $9.99 bottle he would drink.

Champagne / sparkling · Video tasting

True Wine Connoisseurs Prosecco, Italy

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$$ standard Italy Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack places True Wine Connoisseurs Prosecco in Italy's cool-climate Prosecco region and finds grapefruit, fig, white peach, and a slight nutmeg note. He calls it refreshing, easy to drink, alive, complex enough to skip the mimosa treatment, and a good example of protected-place sparkling wine made affordably by tank method.

Other / blend · Video tasting

Tussock Jumper Merlot, USA / California

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$$ standard USA Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack's captured note is direct: "Mack ranks the broad USA / California Merlot label last before tasting because it lacks producer and place specificity, but the wine smells better than expected. He finds it solid and real, though hot and not fully balanced, and upgrades it from a label D to a C-minus after tasting.". The tone is quick, personal, and more about the moment of the bottle than a formal grid of fruit, acid, and tannin. There is no separate score attached here, so the note has to carry the whole read.

Pinot Noir · Video tasting

Underwood Pinot Noir 2019, Oregon

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $ budget USA Bon Appetit Budget pick
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

This lands as a holiday-table bottle, the kind you open when the meal and the people matter. Mack's captured note, "For a large Thanksgiving, Mack wants enough affordable wine for a crowd and chooses Oregon Pinot Noir. He likes the New World fruit crossed with Old World earthy character and says the $14 bottle overdelivers enough to buy by the mixed case.", points less to technical tasting notes and more to occasion. There is no separate score attached here, so the note has to carry the whole read.

Other / blend · Video tasting

Utah: I/G Winery Petite Sirah 2018

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$$ standard Other / mixed regions Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack says I/G Winery Petite Sirah smells like Petite Sirah: plummy, peppery, dry meat rub, vanilla, and nutmeg. It is good quality and not overpowering, but at $65 he wants more oomph and tannin, and says the local fruit cost is not reflected enough in the glass.

Champagne / sparkling · Video tasting

Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $ budget Italy Bon Appetit Budget pick
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack explains that Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore means a higher-quality Prosecco made under protected-name rules, largely from Glera. He sees mousse and carbonation, gets peach and nectarine, calls it refined within Prosecco's own purpose, and says it is festive, fun, everyday luxury, and a strong value at the party-starting job.

Other / blend · Video tasting

Vermont: Snow Farm Vineyards Snow Trip

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$$ standard Other / mixed regions Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack describes Snow Farm Snow Trip as cloudy, unfined, unfiltered natural or low-intervention wine that looks like cider or sour beer. It smells like spiced applesauce and tomato juice, tastes bland and watered down, but he sees the style as a welcome bridge for sour-beer drinkers.

Champagne / sparkling · Video tasting

Veuve Clicquot 1973 Champagne

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$$$ premium France Bon Appetit Collector / benchmark bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack worries the 1973 Veuve is slightly corked, with wet cardboard under the aromas, pear juice from a can, butterscotch kombucha, and old white wine past its prime. He treats it as educational, showing caramelization and age after the wine's best life has passed.

Champagne / sparkling · Video tasting

Veuve Clicquot 1982 Champagne

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$$$ premium France Bon Appetit Collector / benchmark bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack says the 1982 Veuve smells like caramel chew and an old closet in the best way, with cocoa nibs or chocolate and only a faint prickling from the bubbles. Primary fruit has faded far back into cooked-down apple and pear, making it drink more like aged wine than Champagne.

Champagne / sparkling · Video tasting

Veuve Clicquot 1993 Champagne

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$$$ premium France Bon Appetit Collector / benchmark bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack finds the 1993 Veuve more butterscotch-like and reduced, with cooked apples, cooked pears, nutmeg, white flowers, caramel apple, and old-white-wine character. The tiny bubbles create a very different experience from younger Champagne, and he says it needs to be drunk quickly.

Champagne / sparkling · Video tasting

Veuve Clicquot 2012 Champagne

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$$$ premium France Bon Appetit Collector / benchmark bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack gets apple, cooked-down onion, caramelized onion, buttery brioche, green apple, apricot, citrus, and hazelnut from the 2012 Veuve. He says it is beginning to show bottle maturity as primary fruit shifts toward nuttier secondary notes.

Champagne / sparkling · Video tasting

Veuve Clicquot Non-Vintage Champagne

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$$$ premium France Bon Appetit Collector / benchmark bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack finds the non-vintage Veuve Clicquot familiar, with marzipan, wet-rock minerality, lemon, lilacs, toastiness, pear, acid, medium-to-full body, and bubbles that re-fizz in the mouth. He calls it standard, basic entry-level Veuve and a great starting point.

Champagne / sparkling · Video tasting

Via de la Plata Cava, Spain

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$$ standard Spain Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack frames Via de la Plata as Spain's answer to Champagne and points to the Cava seal as a quality guarantee. He sees pale straw color and finds straw, hay, apple, quince, and a little oxidative caramel-apple maturity; for the price, he calls it amazing and a strong Champagne alternative at a fraction of the cost.

Italian red · Video tasting

Villa Medoro Montepulciano d'Abruzzo 2018

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$ value Italy Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack places this in Italy and reads the Montepulciano d'Abruzzo as plum, blackberry, leather, high tannin, and bright acid. He sees enough structure for beef stew or steak and says it has longevity for an under-$15 bottle.

Other / blend · Video tasting

Vina Borgia Garnacha, Spain

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $ budget Spain Bon Appetit Budget pick
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack identifies Vina Borgia as Spanish Garnacha and finds that it looks and smells like wine rather than chemicals, with a little sulfur and hot oil. The alcohol reads hot at 14.5%, but the wine is dry, correctly structured, and in line with Spain's strong under-$15 value reputation, earning a big thumbs up.

Other / blend · Video tasting

Vinchingo The Violet Swell Carignan, Redwood Valley / California

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$$ standard USA Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack's captured note is direct: "Mack reads the clear bottle and no-capsule presentation as natural-wine signals and goes in with high hopes. He finds cherry, mint, wood spice, freshness, and sound natural-wine character without going off the deep end, calling it a cool B-plus wine.". The tone is quick, personal, and more about the moment of the bottle than a formal grid of fruit, acid, and tannin. There is no separate score attached here, so the note has to carry the whole read.

Rose · Video tasting

Virginia: King Family Winery Roseland 2021, Chardonnay / Viognier / Petit Manseng

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$$ standard USA Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack finds King Family Roseland aromatic, honeydew-like, juicy, flavorful, Juicy Fruit-like, textured, dry, and not sweet. He says the blend of Viognier aromatics, Petit Manseng acid, and Chardonnay body works, and calls Virginia one of the most impressive and exciting wine regions in the tasting.

Rose · Video tasting

Von Winning Pinot Noir Rose / Spatburgunder Rose, Germany

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$ value Other / mixed regions Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack places Von Winning in Germany's Pfalz and explains Pinot Noir as Spatburgunder. He gets strawberry, lemon custard, ripe roundness, no residual sugar, and a comforting petrol-like mechanical-shop note, calling it a good and fun example of a German rose category you do not often see.

Champagne / sparkling · Video tasting

Von Winning Sekt Riesling, Germany

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$$ standard Other / mixed regions Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack uses Von Winning Sekt to explain Germany's everyday sparkling-wine culture and Riesling-based Sekt. He sees pale yellow color and gets brioche, caramel apple, confectionary sweetness, and classic Riesling petrol, calling the wine fascinating while noting that import costs likely make it feel expensive for American buyers.

Rose · Video tasting

VRAC Rose 2020, Mediterranean France

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $ budget France Bon Appetit Budget pick
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack sees VRAC Rose as pale and properly rose-colored, but the nose reads a little fishy with raspberry. The wine is dry, alcohol-heavy, light on fruit, and uncertain because the broad 12-to-14% alcohol range seems to land toward the heavier side, leaving him with a thinking-emoji reaction.

Chenin Blanc · Video tasting

Wade Cellars Chenin Blanc / California White Wine 2019, Dwyane Wade

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$$ standard USA Bon Appetit Celebrity wine
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack treats Three by Wade as a 2019 California white wine built mostly from Chenin Blanc, a Loire grape popular with wine people. He gets honeydew melon, honeysuckle, jelly-bean fruit, possible sweetness on the nose, great acid, ripe honeydew, and chamomile. He calls it a great American Chenin Blanc expression, praises the Jason Pahlmeyer partnership, names it his overall favorite, and says it is balling.

Other / blend · Video tasting

West Virginia: Stone Road Vineyard Headlight White

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$$ standard USA Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack says Stone Road Headlight White smells like grapefruit-lemonade soda but tastes like soap, with sweetness, no acid, flabbiness, and flatness. He links the overpowering fragrance to mandarin orange Muscat and says some people might enjoy it, but he cannot.

Other / blend · Video tasting

Wineberry Domaine Le Garrigon Cotes-du-Rhone 2019

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $ budget France Bon Appetit Budget pick
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack points out Wineberry's South of France origin and listed blend of Grenache, Carignan, and Syrah. He gets dark color, spice, nutmeg, cinnamon, heavy almost syrupy texture, bitter chocolate, and a mousy funk he likes, calling it a step up from traditional boxed wine even though it tastes more New World than place-specific Cotes-du-Rhone.

Wisconsin: Harbor Ridge Winery Knocking Heads Red

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$$ standard Other / mixed regions Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack is excited by Harbor Ridge Knocking Heads Red on the nose: pepper, spice, jam, and a pretty, awesome aroma. The wine is better than expected and has Cabernet Franc greenness, but the residual sugar catches him off guard and he thinks it would be more enjoyable drier.

Rose · Video tasting

Wolffer Estate Summer in a Bottle Rose, Long Island

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$ value USA Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack starts with Wolffer Estate Summer in a Bottle from Long Island and sees a pale salmon color. He gets cotton candy, stone fruit, peaches, apricots, wet sea stone, a yellow-Skittle citrus hint, grapefruit on the palate, and calls it refreshing, electric, tasty, and spot-on for $19.99.

Wyoming: Jackson Hole Winery Old Faithful Sparkling Wine

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$$ standard Other / mixed regions Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack says Jackson Hole Old Faithful smells like apple cider and dough, with fine bubbles and a more vintage-like feel. He would drink it, likes the low mousse because the Chardonnay essence comes through, and finds green apple, Asian pear, and elegance in the Wyoming-bottled Sonoma fruit.

Cabernet / Bordeaux · Video tasting

Yao Family Wines Napa Valley Cabernet Sauvignon 2018, Yao Ming

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$$$ premium USA Bon Appetit Celebrity wine
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack sees bright, clear, resilient color in Yao Family Napa Cabernet and gets cassis, raspberry, pencil shavings, plum, vanilla, oak expression, and real tannin structure. He calls it a good California Cabernet and quality wine, but at $98 he wants more detail on vineyard sourcing; he sees it as perfect for steak and grilling.

Cabernet / Bordeaux · Video tasting

Yellow Tail Cabernet Sauvignon

Mack Mack thought captured
👎🏾 Thumbs down from Mack notes $$ value Australia Bon Appetit Pushed back
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack recognizes Yellow Tail's role in making Australian wine huge in the U.S., but the glass itself reads too polished to him. He finds anise, blackberry, boysenberry, bubblegum, sweetness, unnatural roundness, and a color that feels engineered.

Chardonnay · Video tasting

Yellow Tail Chardonnay 2019

Mack Mack thought captured
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes $$ value Australia Bon Appetit Episode bottles
Mack's Thoughts Complete Mack thought

Mack notes that Yellow Tail Chardonnay is inexpensive and widely available. He finds lots and lots of vanilla, sweet-smelling peach, fruit-cocktail richness, ripe sweetness, and a soft-drink-like appeal, saying he can understand why people like it even though it reads like lollipops and cream soda.
